races.race_name: Wyvis Roofing EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: North Star Kodi
runners.spotlight: In need of the experience when third in a 6f maiden here 12 days ago; should be more streetwise today but the runner-up in that race has been well beaten since and dropping to 5f isn't sure to suit.
runners.comment: Green when 3rd over 6f here 12 days ago; 2nd there has been well beaten since; 5f a query

runners.name: Or Another
runners.spotlight: Looked a big threat 1f out on his Pontefract debut four weeks ago (5f, good) but was unable to sustain his effort; he should be more streetwise under pressure this time and he is a strong contender for his in-form stable.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise when 3rd at Pontefract last month; open to improvement; key player

runners.name: Sale Shark
runners.spotlight: 52,000euros yearling; tenth foal; closely related to 7f AW 2yo winner Jacinth (RPR 84), half-brother to six winners including Ekhtiyaar (Group-placed 6f including 2yo; 116), New Providence (5f/6f including 2yo/Group 3; 110) and Dick Datchery (6f including 2yo; 84); dam unplaced 1m-1m2f; stable's 2yos are going well and he is a newcomer of note.
runners.comment: 52,000gns yearling with seven winning siblings; yard's 2yos going well; a newcomer of note

runners.name: Jaijai
runners.spotlight: Weakened in the closing stages when sixth of ten in an Ascot novice (6f, good) 18 days ago; this looks easier and although he is bred to need further, he's still of serious interest.
runners.comment: 6th of ten behind a smart prospect at Ascot (6f, good) 18 days ago; should be more to come

runners.name: Carrow Road
runners.spotlight: 17,000gns yearling, £40,000 breeze-up 2yo; by Caturra; fifth foal; half-brother to winners Manila Scouse (5f/6f including 2yo; RPR 95) and Tasever (7f/1m AW; 76); dam well held in two runs but from a good family; stable not associated with winning 2yo newcomers but he's bred to be sharp and is worth a market check.
runners.comment: £40,000 breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to two winners; market should be revealing

runners.name: Rbflying
runners.spotlight: Dropped away to finish sixth of the eight runners, beaten 12l, in a 5f maiden (good ground) at Thirsk 47 days ago; needs to leave that run well behind to feature.
runners.comment: Dropped right out when 6th at Thirsk 47 days ago; needs to leave that well behind

races.race_name: One Stop Roofing Supplies Ltd Restricted Novice Stakes (For horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bearish
runners.spotlight: Didn't have to match the form of last year's promising debut at Newmarket to see off Pepsea and four others at Thirsk last month; 7lb worse off with Pepsea today but may still uphold the placings; the one to beat.
runners.comment: Beat Pepsea at Thirsk on last month's reappearance; 2yo debut form was even better

runners.name: Pepsea
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed the run of the race when second of six on his Thirsk debut (1m, good) 47 days ago, unable to fend off Bearish's late thrust; 7lb better off with his old rival and could take a step forward.
runners.comment: Second to Bearish on last month's Thirsk debut (1m); 7lb better off for 0.75l today

runners.name: Lavatera
runners.spotlight: 6,000gns yearling, resold 17,000gns yearling; sixth foal; dam once-raced (RPR 69) sister to 7f winner/1,000 Guineas third Moth and 12.5f Listed winner Dirgam, half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Hearthstead Maison; trainer is also responsible for Alterity.
runners.comment: 17,000gns yearling; dam a sister to 1000 Guineas third Moth; trainer also runs Alterity

runners.name: Ice Show
runners.spotlight: In need of the experience when fifth of 12 at Newcastle (1m) on last month's debut; can do better but such progress is essential; trainer also runs Sogniamo.
runners.comment: In need of the experience when fifth at Newcastle last month (1m); should do better

runners.name: Togeather Forever
runners.spotlight: Eleventh foal; half-brother to winners Ralston Road (1m2f-1m6f including Listed; RPR 110), Carbon Dating (1m3f-1m6f; 110), Wickywickywheels (1m3f-1m5f including UAE Listed; 104) and Krissy (1m1f-1m4f; 76); dam unraced; should have a future but perhaps further down the line; hooded for debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to four winners (three useful) but probably a longer-term prospect

runners.name: Alterity
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly; easy to back and ended up a well-beaten fifth of 12 at Newmarket on her debut last July (7f, good to soft); can leave that initial effort well behind her this year.
runners.comment: Smart pedigree; last summer's Newmarket debut wasn't without hope; can do better this year

runners.name: Sogniamo
runners.spotlight: 25-1 and finished a well-beaten last of eight in one run for Marcus Tregoning last summer (7f, good to firm); today's trip more suitable and worth a market check starting out for a new stable (same owners).
runners.comment: Well beaten in one run for Marcus Tregoning last summer (7f); bred to do much better

runners.name: Tricky Tel
runners.spotlight: Began his career with a pair of 5f wins at Chester last May; progress stalled last summer but he returned from a 223-day absence with a good run back at Chester three weeks ago, third of 12 in a 6f handicap on good ground (wide draw); looks on a good mark but can he deliver away from Chester?
runners.comment: Two 2yo wins and his promising reappearance were all at Chester; can he deliver elsewhere?

runners.name: Siouxpersonic
runners.spotlight: 5f AW win for Ed Bethell; good start for her new connections when a close second of ten at Ripon last month (6f, good; first two clear); up 3lb but she can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Promising stable debut when second at Ripon last month (6f); up 3lb but commands respect

runners.name: Falcon Queen
runners.spotlight: Similar form when finishing fourth in three Dundalk maidens (5f-7f) for K J Condon; sold for 22,000gns last month; open to further progress now handicapping.
runners.comment: Similar form in three Dundalk maidens for K Condon; sold 22,000gns since latest; unexposed

runners.name: Watchdog
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a pair of Class 6 wins on AW (7f/6f); his progress has continued back on turf, making all in 6f handicaps at Redcar and Doncaster; another 5lb higher today and also steps up into a Class 4 but he is still more appealing than many.
runners.comment: Progressed well since December; rises in class here but another bold bid is expected
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Sierra Sands
runners.spotlight: Promise as a 2yo, including a Catterick win (7f, good); not beaten far on his seasonal/handicap debut last month but he took a backwards step 25 days ago (reportedly lost action); drops to 6f for the first time and could still have a bigger run in him.
runners.comment: All races at 7f; reappearance was promising and can be given a pass for last time; chance

runners.name: Back To Me
runners.spotlight: 2-2 on AW, including a 7f novice at Southwell on his stable debut in April; only midfield in a 7f handicap at Wetherby three weeks later; drops back in trip with further improvement possible.
runners.comment: Unplaced in both turf starts but 2-2 on AW (6f/7f); drops back in trip; not yet exposed
runners.owner: Castle Racing Co Ltd & Mps Racing Ltd

runners.name: Douwantitrightnow
runners.spotlight: Her third 2yo run represented a jolt of improvement but she failed to build on it when last of four on her handicap debut in February (33-1); a 1lb drop in the weights is not enough to tempt this time.
runners.comment: 33-1 when last of four on her handicap debut; down in trip with a point to prove

runners.name: March Ahead
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a Carlisle fillies' novice (6f, good to firm) last summer when trained by Kevin Ryan; the handicapper looked in charge in two nurseries in the autumn and she may need a bit more weight off her back; first run for Bryan Smart (same owner).
runners.comment: 6f novice win before unplaced in two nurseries last year; new yard; tough mark

runners.name: Shipshape
runners.spotlight: Running well since upped to 1m6f, recording form figures of 212 on his last three starts; no obvious reason he wouldn't go well again and he is travelling a long way for this assignment.
runners.comment: Improving as his stamina is drawn out; should give it another good shot

runners.name: Raulin
runners.spotlight: Three wins over 1m2f last season; unplaced twice this year but dropped 4lb for his troubles and this represents an ease in grade; stamina for 1m5f not assured.
runners.comment: On a good mark and dropping in grade but he has to prove his stamina for 1m5f

runners.name: Furhaan
runners.spotlight: Made a winning handicap debut for Ed Walker off this mark last September (extended 1m3f, heavy); well short of that level in three runs for his new connections and he could prove vulnerable again.
runners.comment: On a winning mark but his success was on heavy and he is yet to shine for Jim Goldie

runners.name: Gentle Warrior
runners.spotlight: Won twice over about 1m6f last September; low-key seasonal return last month but he fared better, albeit beaten at a short price, at Nottingham 18 days ago; shouldn't be too far away.
runners.comment: Two wins at around 1m6f last September; second (of 4) last time; shouldn't be too far away
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXV & E Burke

runners.name: Ludo's Landing
runners.spotlight: Dipped into Class 5 company at Newcastle in March and won comfortably (extended 1m4f); two disappointing runs have followed and he now has a point to prove; has run well in two course visits, at least.
runners.comment: Two disappointing runs have followed March's Class 5 success; has run well here before

runners.name: Binadham
runners.spotlight: Good efforts on his last three starts, not quite able to peg back Law Of Average's advantage when second at Bath four weeks ago (5f, firm); 2lb better off today and he should give his running again.
runners.comment: Closely matched with Law Of Average on latest Bath run; should go well again

runners.name: Oriental Prince
runners.spotlight: In good order on AW over the winter, going close off this mark twice following a Class 4 win in November; returning to turf not a bother and he can't be discounted.
runners.comment: In good form on AW over the winter; absent 131 days; fine on fast turf; not discounted

runners.name: Tiva
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back 5f wins last August saw her soar up the weights; struggled in Listed company on her final two runs last year and she was again well held on her seasonal return at Ripon last month; down in class today and a better run is expected.
runners.comment: Stiff tasks following back-to-back 5f wins last August; better expected today

runners.name: Classy Al
runners.spotlight: Does plenty wrong during his races but it hasn't stopped him from winning seven times, two of them over C&D; running himself back into form this year but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins on his record; looks awkward but capable on his day; others safer this time

runners.name: Law Of Average
runners.spotlight: Progressive 3yo but his form headed south last year; back to winning ways when making all at Bath (5f, firm) four weeks ago; his head carriage has always been a bit ungainly but he's tough to pass when on song; major player despite the 4lb rise.
runners.comment: Fast front-runner when on song; can confirm Bath placings with Binadham; big chance

runners.name: Vingegaard
runners.spotlight: He ran his best race for current connections when third of five at Catterick five weeks ago (6f, good; blinkers first time); dropped 2lb but suspicion he will remain vulnerable.
runners.comment: Best run for new connections when 3rd at Catterick five weeks ago; this demands even more

runners.name: Saxophonist
runners.spotlight: Bettered his novice/maiden form when third of seven in a Doncaster handicap in April (1m, good to firm); that form looks solid and he remains capable of better.
runners.comment: Doncaster 3rd last month looks solid form; open to improvement and he's the one to beat

runners.name: Light Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Unable to build on her debut promise in two runs since, albeit in the face of a still task at Ascot last time; makes her handicap debut in an uncompetitive race and she could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Not built on debut promise yet but goes handicapping in an uncompetitive race

runners.name: Porth Eilian
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to connections' smart Point Lynas; needs to step up on the form of her three runs in fillies' novice/maiden company but she may do just that.
runners.comment: Needs to improve for switch to handicaps but there are grounds for expecting much better
runners.owner: Julie & David Martin, Dan Hall & Partner

runners.name: Sanafi Zabeel
runners.spotlight: 1m1f winner in Germany; enough promise in his two course runs for his new stable to think he would be winning sooner rather than later; Paul Mulrennan takes over today; interesting.
runners.comment: 1m1f winner in Germany; two encouraging runs for new yard; Paul Mulrennan takes over today

runners.name: Union Island
runners.spotlight: Won two of his last five starts (with and without headgear), scrambling home at Leicester (1m, good to firm) 18 days ago; a 2lb rise not excessive and he has a good strike-rate for one of his rating; chance.
runners.comment: Eight-time winner, including two this year; conditions should suit; still feasibly treated

runners.name: Donna Nook
runners.spotlight: Her losing run stands at 18 and she disappointed as favourite at Beverley two weeks ago; on the plus side, she went desperately close at Ayr (behind Starliner) three weeks ago and won't be far away if back at that level.
runners.comment: On losing run and disappointed last time but her earlier Ayr second brings her into it

runners.name: Barley
runners.spotlight: Not a prolific winner but he is operating off a reduced mark and there were more encouraging signs dropped to a Class 5 at Thirsk 11 days ago; another 3lb lower today and minor money could be on the cards.
runners.comment: More encouragement dropped to a Class 5 latest; dangerous mark; place claims

runners.name: Eve's Boy
runners.spotlight: Good second at Thirsk on his seasonal return last month; sent off favourite at Ayr on the strength of it but could only manage sixth of the seven runners behind Starliner (Donna Nook second); cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: Promising 2nd on seasonal return but behind two of these at Ayr latest; headgear back now

runners.name: Starliner
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of his career-low mark when beating Donna Nook at Ayr (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago; not so good at Thirsk last time and he is not the easiest to predict.
runners.comment: Beat two of these at Ayr three weeks ago but failed to fire at Thirsk last time; not solid
runners.owner: David Orr & Miss L A Perratt

runners.name: Yaaser
runners.spotlight: Smooth win at Newcastle (7f) in December and now rated 2lb lower; that is a result of beating just one horse home in four subsequent starts; jury out for now.
runners.comment: Struggled since his AW win in December (7f); good mark but too much to prove

races.race_name: Stock Exe Building Supplies Mares' "National Hunt" Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lady Kara
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt over hurdles when scoring by 9l in a Hereford novice (2m3f, good) two weeks ago; that was at 1-2 in a five-runner race but her main market rival was a clear second and she hit a personal best with that win; big player again under a penalty.
runners.comment: Off the mark when landing the odds with 9l win at Hereford; big player again under penalty

runners.name: Babs
runners.spotlight: Won on bumper debut (good) in October but she found things tough in Listed events in next two starts before a well-held fifth hurdling debut at Warwick (2m, good) this month; still early days and should have a future over jumps but she needs improvement.
runners.comment: Won a bumper on debut in October but she's not gone on from that; needs improvement

runners.name: Frenati
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly; big improvement when winning a Stratford bumper (2m, soft) in March and she took another major step forward when a close second here (2m1f, good) last month; looks interesting now switched to hurdling and she has a good jumps pedigree.
runners.comment: Bumper winner and she looks an interesting recruit to hurdling; needs a close look

runners.name: Princess Of Ballea
runners.spotlight: Three encouraging efforts in bumpers but she has taken a strong hold at times and her form isn't strong; out of a 2m hurdle winner but she needs plenty of progress on this switch to jumping.
runners.comment: Some encouraging efforts in bumpers but she needs improvement on hurdling debut

runners.name: Lady Litigator
runners.spotlight: Mixed results in four bumper runs but she won on Newbury debut (2m, good to soft) in December and was runner-up at Wincanton (1m7f, good) last month; out of an unraced sister to a useful hurdler and she needs a close look on this switch to jumps.
runners.comment: Newbury bumper winner and she's in the mix on hurdling debut

runners.name: Whodunit
runners.spotlight: Finished down the field in two bumpers this spring, most recently when eighth of 12 here (2m1f, good) last month; wasn't beaten far in that race but she still needs a transformation on this switch to hurdling.
runners.comment: Down the field in two bumpers this spring and needs a transformation on hurdling debut

runners.name: Bontessony Doll
runners.spotlight: 100-1 and she didn't get very far in a good-ground C&D novice event (unseated at first) on recent debut.
runners.comment: 100-1 and she didn't get very far in a C&D novice (unseated at first) on recent debut

runners.name: G Whizz
runners.spotlight: Some ability in points and a bumper but she started her hurdling career with heavy defeat at Warwick (2m, good; 125-1) three weeks ago; opposable.
runners.comment: Some ability in points and a bumper but was tailed off on hurdling debut at Warwick

runners.name: Amhranai
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in three bumpers for two different yards, with latest at Kempton (2m, good) in March; can only be watched on hurdling/stable debut.
runners.comment: Tailed off in three bumpers for two different yards; no appeal on hurdling/stable debut

runners.name: Minnie Belle
runners.spotlight: Beaten 37l, 52l and 43l in three hurdle runs and last two have been at Stratford (2m, good) this spring; no appeal.
runners.comment: Beaten 37l, 52l and 43l in three hurdle runs; no appeal

races.race_name: Totnes And Bridgetown Races Company Ltd. Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Thickthorn Tom
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden but he's been placed in the last two of his three chase runs including when a clear second at Fontwell (2m3f, good; tongue-tie added) 13 days ago; looks interesting off the same mark here and he should have more to offer over fences; leading contender.
runners.comment: Unexposed chaser who was a clear second off this mark at Fontwell latest; leading claims

runners.name: It's A Breeze
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles but he was runner-up off 1lb higher at Wincanton (3m) in March; fell four out on chase debut at Fontwell (2m3f, good) last month but he was close up in fourth when he departed; has claims if he can avoid any mishaps this time and he's in the mix for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Fell four out on chase debut at Fontwell but he was close up at the time; dangerous

runners.name: Roi Du Roume
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who is 0-5 over hurdles but he's run respectably to reach the frame in two handicaps at Perth (3m/2m4f, good) this spring; mark hasn't budged but his latest run may have come too soon and he should have a future at this new discipline; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Irish point winner who is still lightly raced and he needs a close look on chase debut

runners.name: Georges Street
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in three of his four points and he's been tailed in four runs over hurdles including a Warwick handicap (3m1f, good) this month; has plenty to prove and he needs a transformation on chase debut; first-time blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much so far and he needs a transformation on chase debut; first-time blinkers

runners.name: Senor Diaz
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden who has been pulled up in both runs over fences (2m3f) and was tailed off back over hurdles at Exeter (2m, good) last time; opposable.
runners.comment: Record of 0-13 and he's been pulled up in both runs over fences; opposable

runners.name: Getaway Tothe Wood
runners.spotlight: Struggled at massive prices in four hurdle runs and same story when pulled up on handicap/chase debut at Leicester (2m4f, soft) in December 2024; on the sidelines since then and can only be watched back from a long absence.
runners.comment: Struggled at massive prices in all five runs and latest was in December 2024; passed over

races.race_name: Hugh Michelmore Memorial Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Rickety Bridge
runners.spotlight: Point winner who was 1-8 over hurdles but he made a smooth transition to chasing when scoring over C&D (good; 13-2) three weeks ago; up 3lb but he travelled well for a long way last time and hit a personal best with that convincing success; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Smooth winner on chase debut over C&D this month and a 3lb rise looks fair; interesting

runners.name: Snipe
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-4 since switched to hunter chases in January and he ran well to finish six of 24 in the Foxhunters' at Aintree (2m5f) last month; on career-high mark back in a handicap but he hasn't been far off his best this year and he has won over 3m1f in the past; respected.
runners.comment: Has done well in hunter chases this year including two wins; respected back in a handicap

runners.name: Time To Bite
runners.spotlight: Admirable veteran who recorded his ninth chase win when making all to score from 2lb out of the weights at Fakenham (3m, good) this month; this is tougher off 4lb higher but he seems as good as ever as an 11yo and three of his wins have come at this track; key player.
runners.comment: Recorded his ninth chase win when making all at Fakenham this month; respected up 4lb

runners.name: Moroder
runners.spotlight: Six-time chase winner who returned to form with a rallying second at Taunton (3m4f, good) last month; only 1lb higher here and has claims if he can build on that back in trip.
runners.comment: Back to form with a second at Taunton (3m4f) and has claims if he can back that up

runners.name: Blackjack Magic
runners.spotlight: Well treated on old form but his last win was in November 2023 and he's generally struggled since then; has something to prove and all of his wins have come on good to soft or slower.
runners.comment: HIs last win was in November 2023 and he's generally struggled since; opposable

races.race_name: Chris Wills Memorial Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Reel Orange
runners.spotlight: Progressive mare who has form figures of 24121 since joining Joe Tizzard and both of those wins have been at Newton Abbot; 5lb higher than for her success over C&D (good) two weeks ago but she still looks feasibly treated and could continue on an upward curve; key player.
runners.comment: Has record of 24121 for Joe Tizzard and both wins have been at Newton Abbot; key player
runners.owner: J M Dare, T Hamlin, J W Snook

runners.name: Ocean Heights
runners.spotlight: Bumper and four-time Flat (1m2f/1m4f) winner who is ideally suited by fast ground; respectable fourth on AW when last seen in February and he's on a workable mark back over hurdles; needs close look for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Ideally suited by fast ground and he's on workable mark back over hurdles; in the mix

runners.name: Makinbecon
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two Irish points last spring but he's not made an impact in four hurdle runs including in a Ffos Las handicap (2m4f, good; 20-1) this month; still has low mileage but he needs to raise his game back in trip.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 6yo but he needs to raise his game back in trip

runners.name: Saucats
runners.spotlight: Ran well to finish a clear fourth in a Taunton handicap (2m, good) last month; has each-way claims if he can back that up but he's not easy to predict and is still a maiden after 21 starts; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Fair fourth at Taunton last time but he's not easy to predict and is now 1-21

runners.name: Getone
runners.spotlight: 7yo who has been beaten 24l or more in his five starts including three over hurdles; this is more realistic on handicap debut but he's taken a strong hold in his last two starts and he needs improvement with hood added.
runners.comment: Still early days but he needs to settle better and find improvement with a hood added

runners.name: Alberts Bay
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete in three of his four Irish points; showed some ability on hurdling debut here (2m5f, heavy) in September 2023 but he's had just two since and has been tailed off in both of them; now goes handicapping but this 9yo has a lot to prove.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced 9yo and he has a lot to prove on handicap debut

runners.name: St Lukes Chelsea
runners.spotlight: Modest form in six Flat runs and he's 0-11 over hurdles; beaten 11l when fifth at Taunton (2m, good; 15-8 favourite) in March but he went close there on his penultimate run and has claims if he recapture that form.
runners.comment: 0-11 over hurdles but he went close at Taunton on penultimate run; not ruled out

runners.name: Scoresby
runners.spotlight: Six-time point winner who made it third-time lucky over hurdles when scoring on his handicap debut at Fontwell (2m3f, good) three weeks ago; up 5lb but that was a smooth success and the runner-up has franked that form since; respected.
runners.comment: Six-time point winner who won on handicap debut at Fontwell this month; respected up 5lb

runners.name: Blazing Gaden
runners.spotlight: Has struggled in all of his six runs including when pulled up in a Stratford handicap (2m, good to firm) ten days ago; 5lb out of the weights this time and he can only be watched.
runners.comment: Has struggled in all six runs and he can only be watched from out of the weights

runners.name: Crab Road
runners.spotlight: 5yo who didn't show much at big prices in five hurdle runs (2m-2m3f) in Ireland; now makes debut for new yard and he needs a transformation on this switch to fast ground.
runners.comment: Didn't show much in six runs in Ireland and he needs a transformation for new yard

races.race_name: Mrs. Pamela Ann Dobbs Birthday Celebration Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Byzantium
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins last June and latest was over C&D (good); no joy in just three runs since but he was in lead when he ran out and crashed through the rails before the second-last at Wincanton (3m, good) last month; has possibilities if he can maintain his focus this time and he's well handicapped on his best form.
runners.comment: Ran out at the second-last at Wincanton but he was in the lead at the time; possibilities

runners.name: American Sniper
runners.spotlight: Has patchy profile but he's well handicapped on his Taunton second (2m3f, good) in December and he may have needed his reappearance run at Hereford this month; this step back up in trip is no problem and he's one to keep an eye on for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Hard to predict but he's on dangerous mark and yard is in good form; not ruled out

runners.name: Chillhi
runners.spotlight: Flat and dual hurdle winner for James Owen last summer but well beaten in three runs for new stable (Flat/hurdles) this spring; now 4lb lower than for his last success but he needs a major revival; blinkers return and visor is removed.
runners.comment: Has struggled for new yard this spring and he needs a major revival

runners.name: Ellerton
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-3 for Joe Tizzard when scoring back from another break at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good; 100-30) two weeks ago; up 5lb but that was a comfortable win from off the pace and this step back up in trip should be fine (first hurdle win was over 2m4f); big player.
runners.comment: 2-3 for Joe Tizzard including a clearcut win here 14 days ago; big player back up in trip

runners.name: Impecunious
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Chris Down last year, with the first at Chepstow (2m3f, good) and latest at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good); well-held fourth of seven on recent stable debut at Worcester (2m5f) but he ran well for a long way and should benefit from that run; could be dangerous if he can get back near best.
runners.comment: Two wins last year and he ran well for long way on recent stable debut; could be dangerous

runners.name: Sam's Amour
runners.spotlight: Triple hurdle winner (2m2f-2m5f) in 2024 but she's struggled in last six runs and was pulled up here (3m2f) three weeks ago; has plenty to prove and needs a big turnaround back in trip.
runners.comment: Three hurdle wins in 2024 but she's struggled in last six runs; lots to prove

races.race_name: Racing Foundation Supports Autism In Racing Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Belgarum
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in his final two runs last year but he returned after wind surgery with a front-running win at Fontwell (2m3f, good; 22-1) this month; this is a different test but he won over fences here (3m2f) last spring and 3lb rise for that last success looks fair; respected.
runners.comment: Returned after wind surgery with a win at Fontwell this month; respected back up in trip

runners.name: Renoir
runners.spotlight: On dangerous mark but he's 1-11 over hurdles and hasn't really fired since returning after wind surgery this spring; has a bit to prove and he's untried beyond 2m5f.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but he hasn't fired in two runs this spring and is untried beyond 2m5f

runners.name: Sea Invasion
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner who made it 3-11 over fences when rallying well to score at Plumpton (3m2f, good) last month; that was a last-gasp win but he's only 1lb higher here and this strong stayer is respected back over hurdles; entered 7.39 at Plumpton on Tuesday.
runners.comment: Last-gasp win over fences at Plumpton (3m2f) last month; entered 7.39 there on Tuesday

runners.name: Clotilda
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler who went close off 1lb lower at Cheltenham (2m1f, good) last April; back over fences in last five starts and she's finished runner-up three times including at Newton Abbot (2m5f) last month; now returns to hurdling at this new trip but she ran respectably over 2m7f on her penultimate run and has possibilities if her stamina holds out; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark back over hurdles and has claims if she copes with this new trip

runners.name: Supreme Yeats
runners.spotlight: Veteran who returned to form when landing a gamble with his smooth success over C&D (good; cheekpieces added) three weeks ago; up 6lb but he beat a clear second last time and he's still unexposed at this sort of trip; big player.
runners.comment: Returned to form with a smooth win over C&D; big player again off 6lb higher
runners.owner: The Old Stag Racing Sy Partnership

races.race_name: Happy Birthday Joe McCabe Claiming Stakes

runners.name: Penny Arcade
runners.spotlight: Sent off second favourite but finished last of four at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft; winner subsequently won the Lily Agnes) on her debut; built on that when second of 12 at Thirsk (5f, good; fourth has won since) 11 days ago, keeping on well; should be suited by this stiff finish and she has leading claims.
runners.comment: Built on her debut when second of 12 at Thirsk (fourth has won since); leading claims

runners.name: Lairy Mary
runners.spotlight: Built on her debut outing when third of nine over C&D (good to soft) on her second start but well held at Thirsk (5f, good) 11 days ago; each-way claims back here in this lower grade.
runners.comment: Third over C&D on second start; held latest but down in grade; each-way contender

runners.name: Tricky Jenny
runners.spotlight: Beaten 12l when fifth of ten on her debut at Musselburgh (5f, soft) but showed more when sixth of eight at Ripon (6f, good) ten days ago; has shown speed and should appreciate this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Showed more over 6f at Ripon on her second start; has shown speed; now drops back in trip

runners.name: Spirit Of Mary
runners.spotlight: Down the field over C&D on her debut and it was the same story over 6f at Ripon ten days ago (good); drops back in trip and much more is needed.
runners.comment: Down the field over C&D then at Ripon (6f); much more is needed

runners.name: Spark In The Coal
runners.spotlight: 22-1 and finished well held on her debut over C&D 23 days ago (good to soft); open to some improvement but others have shown more.
runners.comment: Well held on her debut over C&D 23 days ago; open to progress but others have shown more

runners.name: Toon Army
runners.spotlight: Well beaten after rearing at the start on his debut at York (6f, good; 40-1) 13 days ago and now drops in trip; this is less competitive but others are preferred.
runners.comment: Well beaten after rearing at the start on his debut at York (6f; 40-1); drops in trip

runners.name: Jimmy You Mushroom
runners.spotlight: 22-1 and finished last of 11 over C&D (good to soft) on recent debut; has since been gelded and may show a bit more on good ground.
runners.comment: 22-1 and finished last of 11 (good to soft) on his debut 23 days ago; since been gelded

runners.name: Tree Wizard
runners.spotlight: Raced wide when beating only one rival home on his debut at Bath (5f, good) 23 days ago; cheekpieces are now applied.
runners.comment: Raced wide when beating only one home on his debut at Bath this month; cheekpieces added

races.race_name: Tigers Trust Restricted Novice Stakes (For horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Dei Gratia Regina
runners.spotlight: Fifth of ten at Bath (5f, good) on last month's debut and she built on that when a 3l fourth at Nottingham (6f, good) 31 days ago;  pedigree suggests this longer trip will suit (dam 7f/1m winner) and has claims.
runners.comment: Built on debut when fourth over 6f at Nottingham; pedigree suggests this trip will suit

runners.name: Sou'wester
runners.spotlight: Dam 6f 2yo winner (RPR 73); ran green but did show ability on his debut at Redcar (7f, good; 40-1) nine days ago; open to progress over this slightly longer trip.
runners.comment: Ran green but did show ability on his debut at Redcar nine days ago; open to progress

runners.name: Confernation
runners.spotlight: April foal; 130,000gns foal, 30,000gns yearling; Sioux Nation colt; third foal; dam runner-up 11.4f turf/1m4f AW (RPR 81), half-sister to 7f/1m Listed winner Doom, out of US 9.5f/1m2f Grade 1 winning half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 winner Sulk; yard has started the season well with their 2yos and he's one to watch in the betting.
runners.comment: 130,000gns foal, 30,000gns yearling; dam runner-up 11.4f turf/1m4f AW; watch the betting

runners.name: Out Of This World
runners.spotlight: February foal; 38,000gns foal, 32,000gns yearling; Space Blues colt; third foal; half-brother to 1m2f AW 2yo winner Vilaine Verlaine (RPR 80); dam 1m 2yo/12.4f AW winner (88), sister to Listed-placed 1m5f winner Galmarley; market should be informative on his debut.
runners.comment: 38,000gns foal, 32,000gns yearling; market informative on debut

runners.name: Bollin Marsha
runners.spotlight: February foal; Wells Farhh Go filly; first foal; dam 1m2f-1m4f winner (RPR 84), half-sister to winners Bollin Joan (1m2f-1m4f) and Bollin Ted (1m2f), out of 1m4f-1m6f winning half-sister to St Leger winner Bollin Eric; pedigree suggests longer trips will suit.
runners.comment: Wells Farhh Go filly; first foal; dam 1m2f-1m4f winner; longer trips may suit
runners.owner: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner

runners.name: Sydney Carton
runners.spotlight: February foal; Ulysses colt; ninth foal; closely related to 1m6f-2m2f winner Land Of Oz (RPR 99), half-brother to winners Environment Amigo (1m6f/16.5f AW; 87) and Charles Darnay (1m4f AW; 73); dam Listed-placed 1m2f winner (100); hooded for debut.
runners.comment: Closely related to 1m6f-2m2f winner Land Of Oz (RPR 99); hooded for his debut

runners.name: Hamdani Mokhater
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to six winners including Al Messila (1m; RPR 87), Muatadel (5f-7.4f including AW; 86), Born Slippy (6f 2yo; 83), Cashla Bay (7f 2yo; 82) and Fard (6f including AW; 72); well beaten on Yarmouth debut (6f, good to firm) 36 days ago and needs more over 7f.
runners.comment: Half-brother to six winners but well beaten on his debut at Yarmouth (6f) 36 days ago

runners.name: Amantha
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival home on last month's debut here over 5f and she was reported to have hung left when eighth of nine here 34 days ago; steps up in trip with much more needed.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two 5f starts here last month; steps up in trip with much more needed

runners.name: Woodstock
runners.spotlight: On a 16-race losing run but down the weights as a result and he was on the heels of the winner when third of 12 at Thirsk (7f) 11 days ago; having his first race here; thereabouts if he takes to it.
runners.comment: On a 16-race losing run but he was on the heels of the winner at Thirsk (7f) 11 days ago

runners.name: Rajapour
runners.spotlight: AW race in December when visored first time is his only win for this yard from 21 attempts; last three starts (switched to cheekpieces; also tongue tied latest) reiterated, however, that he's still likely to be competitive if he takes to this track.
runners.comment: 1-21 for this yard but he's likely to be competitive if he takes to this track

runners.name: Thats My Boy Luke
runners.spotlight: 8.4f second or third last spring on his three outings at this track, twice from the front; two 1m wins at Ripon last summer, showing form which has not been recaptured this season but he has a competitive mark.
runners.comment: Last year's form has not been recaptured but he has a competitive mark

runners.name: Straight A
runners.spotlight: Mid-division on both starts here (2024); solid AW campaign has been carried over to turf with seconds at Doncaster (7f) and Redcar (1m) on last two outings, beaten a nose last time, and those put him firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Back to turf with seconds at Doncaster (7f) and Redcar (1m); beaten a nose last time

runners.name: Sunny Orange
runners.spotlight: 4-15 at Beverley, with the wins all in Class 6; the most recent win was last month (8.4f) but he's taken two backward steps since and most form in the last two years suggests a supporting role.
runners.comment: 4-15 here, the wins in Class 6; most form in the last two years suggests a supporting role

runners.name: Tropez Power
runners.spotlight: Gained sole turf success in 2021 and his last AW win was in April 2024; some minor honours over C&D and he has another career-low mark, but others have far more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Last won in April 2024; some minor honours over C&D and he has another career-low mark

runners.name: In A Hurry
runners.spotlight: Second in a C&D maiden on sole appearance here; Thirsk 7f maiden win last June preceded two handicap placings but she's been right out of form in her three starts since, one this season.
runners.comment: Two handicap placings but she's been right out of form in her three starts since

runners.name: Coolree
runners.spotlight: Has won here twice already this month, taking his course record to 112311; all those races were over the extended 1m, so this race asks a slightly different question but he grabbed control well over 1f out last time, is back in career-best form and highly respected.
runners.comment: Record here is 112311, all over the extended 1m but he's highly respected

runners.name: This Farh
runners.spotlight: Unraced here; all three wins have been over about 7f, the latest at Leicester last month; 6f probably explains a lesser show last time and he has a chance judged on peak efforts.
runners.comment: Leicester 7f win last month; 6f probably explains a lesser show last time

runners.name: Judicature
runners.spotlight: AW novice winner as 3yo; 0-6 in handicaps and although this season's two runs give some encouragement, the best of those (a narrow defeat over Carlisle's stiff 7f last time) was on soft ground.
runners.comment: Best handicap run (a narrow defeat over Carlisle's stiff 7f last time) was on soft ground

runners.name: Harswell Ruby
runners.spotlight: Won at Thirsk in April and short-headed for third by Rajapour there 11 days ago, both over 1m; runner-up twice over C&D last season, she has to be considered each-way.
runners.comment: Competitive lately and 2nd twice over C&D last season, she has to be considered each-way

runners.name: Miss Willows
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who bookended last season with 7f wins at Catterick and Redcar; unraced here and this season did not start so well, when 11th of 14 at Thirsk, so others make appeal.
runners.comment: Front-runner who won first and last starts in 2025; this season did not start so well

runners.name: Yermanthere
runners.spotlight: 3-39 overall, last winning in August 2024; close C&D third last May on sole start here; previous campaigns suggest he's best watched first time out.
runners.comment: 3-39 overall, last winning in August 2024, and he's probably best watched first time out

runners.name: Kitsune Power
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in 2022 but his latest success came nearly two years ago; third on his first two outings this season (1m4f) and did enough to suggest he's still in form when fourth of six at Redcar (1m6f, good) 20 days ago; drop back in trip may not be ideal.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2022 but nearly two years without a win; fair efforts over further this year

runners.name: Triple Force
runners.spotlight: Had a productive time of things in 2025 when the visor went on, winning on four occasions, including over this C&D last June; well beaten on the AW at Newcastle in November and he was too free on his comeback run at Ripon (1m2f, good) 19 days ago; may take a step forward from that but will need to.
runners.comment: Had a productive time of things in the visor last year but well beaten on last two starts

runners.name: Dawn Of Liberation
runners.spotlight: His five wins include one over C&D last May; below his best on his first two starts this year but took a step back in the right direction when a 2l fifth of 12 at Ripon (1m2f, good) 19 days ago; contender.
runners.comment: Five wins include one over C&D last May; took a step back in the right direction last time

runners.name: Mao Shang Wong
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark when winning at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in March and followed up at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) on his next start; however, he's been well held on his last three starts and remains 3lb higher than for his latest success.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle and Doncaster in March; however, well held on his next three starts

runners.name: Hibernate
runners.spotlight: Made virtually all in a five-runner race at Yarmouth (1m1f, good to firm) last month and followed up in another small field at Bath (11.5f, firm) 15 days ago; up 6lb for last time and steps up in grade, but can't be dismissed in his bid for the hat-trick.
runners.comment: Won in small fields at Yarmouth and Bath on his last two starts; up another 6lb

runners.name: Endless Whisper
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but she was runner-up at Doncaster over 1m4f on two of her three starts last summer (tailed off on the AW on the other one); returned from 258 days off with a solid effort there over 1m2f in March and has claims on her first start since.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two of her three starts last year and posted a solid effort on her return

runners.name: Titian
runners.spotlight: Won an amateur riders' race off a mark 2lb higher than today's at York (1m2f, good) last June but he didn't match that level on his six subsequent starts; never-dangerous sixth of ten at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) when last seen 181 days ago and has since undergone his second wind operation; each-way claims on his return.
runners.comment: Won off 2lb higher at York last year; has had a wind operation since last seen in November

races.race_name: Connexin's Full Fibre For All Handicap

runners.name: Hostelry
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner, including C&D, and she's dropped a long way in the weights; latest fourth of 14 over C&D was a welcome return to form and the form is working out well; one to take seriously with Kaiya Fraser back on board.
runners.comment: C&D winner on a lowly mark; latest C&D fourth working out well; one to take seriously

runners.name: Princess Vivi
runners.spotlight: First real signs of promise when fourth in a couple of minor AW events in March; not at her best 16 days ago and she is not a solid option back on turf.
runners.comment: Minor AW form in March; not at best 16 days ago; more to prove back on turf

runners.name: Onemorenomore
runners.spotlight: Should be making a big impact at this level and he is 1lb lower than for his Southwell win (7f) in November; still to convince he wants this far though.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but he is still to convince that he wants this far

runners.name: Ring Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW winner but 0-22 on turf; ran poorly behind Penny Ghent at Ripon ten days ago and others have more obvious claims.
runners.comment: Five AW wins but 0-22 on turf; ran poorly behind Penny Ghent ten days ago; opposable

runners.name: Berning Hot
runners.spotlight: Her sole success came on turf and her recent Kempton second (1m) was comparable form; could go well back on the grass.
runners.comment: Good second on AW (1m) latest; only win came on turf (7f, good); not ruled out

runners.name: Penny Ghent
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 27th attempt when running away with a Ripon handicap ten days ago (1m, good); well in under a penalty and she is ideally drawn to attack again but it remains to be seen if she can repeat the feat.
runners.comment: Made all for easy win at Ripon 10 days ago (1m); well drawn to attack; can she back it up?

runners.name: Poet's Dawn
runners.spotlight: Rolled back the years with a front-running 1m2f win at Ripon ten days ago; only has a 4lb penalty to deal with and Jake Dickson is good value for his 7lb claim; unlikely to get a soft time of things up front though.
runners.comment: Made all to win 1m2f handicap at Ripon ten days ago; penalty to shoulder; others stronger

runners.name: Sisterandbrother
runners.spotlight: Gained his first win for this stable at Doncaster (1m4f, good) this month and ran well at Southwell (1m3f, AW) six days ago; still fairly treated off this 2lb higher mark and has good claims.
runners.comment: Won at Doncaster this month and ran well at Southwell six days ago; now 2lb higher

runners.name: Regal Glory
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 38 starts came over 7f at Lingfield in 2022 but she has posted some good efforts this year, including when runner-up at Wolverhampton (1m6f, AW) last month; however, she was disappointing at Southwell (2m, AW) 29 days ago and needs to bounce back returned to this shorter trip.
runners.comment: 1-38 but she has posted some good efforts this year; needs to bounce back from last time

runners.name: Freddy Robinson
runners.spotlight: Sole win on the Flat came here over 1m2f in June 2024 and he's finished runner-up over this C&D on his last two outings; no reason why he can't go well again.
runners.comment: Sole win on the Flat came here and he's been second over C&D on his last two outings

runners.name: Off Spin
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden but he posted a solid effort when third of 11 in a higher grade than this at Ripon (1m2f, good) ten days ago; can make his presence felt if staying this longer trip (unraced beyond 1m3f).
runners.comment: 0-13 but posted a solid effort in a higher grade than this last time; steps up in trip
runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor

runners.name: Rajawail
runners.spotlight: 25-race maiden who was well held here over 8.5f (good to soft) 23 days ago; steps back up in trip and others are preferred.
runners.comment: 0-25 and well held here over 8.5f last time; back up in trip; others preferred

runners.name: Bruce Banner
runners.spotlight: Made a winning yard debut at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) last April and he's since won over hurdles at Musselburgh in November; bounced back from a lesser effort when second of six at Redcar (1m6f, good) 20 days ago and won't mind this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Bounced back from a lesser effort when second at Redcar (1m6f) last time; back in trip

races.race_name: Racing Again This Saturday Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: King Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: Never involved on reappearance but he'd been off for ten months and he could easily do better today, having been placed in just two C&D runs, including when going close in this race in 2024 (under Lewis Chalkley who switches to So Grateful); not ruled out.
runners.comment: Close 2nd in the 2024 running; better for recent comeback; not ruled out

runners.name: Thornaby Pearl
runners.spotlight: Both wins on soft but has useful form on good to firm; has yet to race on this course but he's versatile track-wise and comes here in good form after finishing close up at Hamilton (5f, good) recently; thereabouts.
runners.comment: Both wins on soft but has form on good to firm; in good form; thereabouts again

runners.name: South Shore
runners.spotlight: Losing run goes back nearly a year but he might have won over C&D last July but for being hampered and is on a handy mark now; a recent Tapeta run (hood left off, now refitted) suggests a good run is imminent.
runners.comment: Unlucky over C&D last July; well treated; looks to be running into form again

runners.name: Amerjeet
runners.spotlight: Sole win on AW and his rare turf handicap runs haven't seen him to best advantage, admittedly when higher in the weights than he is now; beaten favourite four times from last six runs though and others higher up the list.
runners.comment: Sole win on AW; no great shakes on turf so far; others higher up the list

runners.name: Desert Master
runners.spotlight: Has run some good races on Tapeta this year and only just failed at Southwell (5f) in April; however, he wasn't in much form on turf in 2025, including in this race, and looks unlikely to break his turf duck.
runners.comment: Went down narrowly on AW latest; unconvincing on turf in 2025 and well held in this race
runners.owner: John And Tony Jarvis And Partner

runners.name: Nordic Glory
runners.spotlight: Multiple AW wins; much less prolific on turf but he was running over 5f on grass for the first time since a 2024 win when getting up near the line at Bath (firm) recently under James Glennon; has an awkward draw but this track may also suit; has to be considered.
runners.comment: Has won last two 5f runs on turf, in 2024 and this month, under J Glennon; considered

runners.name: So Grateful
runners.spotlight: Usually leads or races prominently; two sprint wins in 2025, the latest over 6f at Thirsk (good to firm; when 3lb higher) last May, but he was quiet towards the end of the campaign and has been off since August; perhaps other days.
runners.comment: 3lb below latest win last May; quiet end to year and off since August; perhaps other days

runners.name: Crafty Spirit
runners.spotlight: An AW winner in 2024 for previous yard but he needs to improve on his turf efforts for new connections last year; lack of a recent run is another negative.
runners.comment: AW winner in 2024; needs improvement on turf efforts in 2025 for new connections

runners.name: Until Dawn
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; two 5f wins on AW; 0-8 on turf but she didn't go down by much when third at Hamilton (5f, good) this month; she produced a much weaker finish there since, though, and her stamina will be tested on this stiffer track; yard won this in 2025.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins on AW; 0-8 on turf; close 3rd at Hamilton but weak finish there 12 days ago
runners.owner: F Reay, S McMillan & S Pedersen

runners.name: Cape Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Not yet off the mark but he was close up in better races than this last spring, including over C&D, and has dropped a long way in the weights after a quiet end to his time with Kevin Ryan; sold £3,000 since; would come right into it if in anything approaching his best form.
runners.comment: Maiden; ran well in good sprints last spring, including over C&D; left K Ryan for £3,000

runners.name: Redwood Queen
runners.spotlight: Has failed to distinguish herself since the controversy generated by her rider's unseat at the last in this race; travelled powerfully into the straight then and was well in command; out of her depth in a Listed race at Killarney last time; previously only a moderate third at Tramore, but in front of Oh So Charming and Hasten Slowly, with War Correspondent pulled up.
runners.comment: Returns to the scene of last year's controversial unseat, clearly risky but could feature

runners.name: Oh So Charming
runners.spotlight: Sole win over hurdles came in a Clonmel handicap last June off 111; has slumped in the ratings and failed to exploit her reduced mark (97) when fifth at Clonmel 13 days ago; however, he is closely matched with Redwood Queen on running in a claimer at Tramore last month.
runners.comment: Closely matched with Redwood Queen on running in a claimer at Tramore, definite chance

runners.name: Hasten Slowly
runners.spotlight: Two of her three wins over hurdles have come at Tramore; however, she was a long way below her best in a similar race to this at that course last month; that run was prefaced by weak form at Dundalk during the winter.
runners.comment: Useful dual-purpose type, a long way below best in a similar race at Tramore last month

runners.name: Plastic Paddy
runners.spotlight: Seven-time AW winner in Britain for Michael Appleby; no success since 2024, and Flat mark has dropped to a lowly 42; jumped badly and pulled up on his hurdling debut at Punchestown two weeks ago.
runners.comment: No recent sign of his best Flat form (seven AW wins), jumped badly on hurdles debut

runners.name: War Correspondent
runners.spotlight: Decent form over fences last autumn, including third in a Punchestown Grade 3 novice; pulled up in a race of this type over hurdles at Tramore last month; better when fourth off his higher chase mark at this venue on latest; hard to win with.
runners.comment: Better chaser than hurdler, pulled up in a similar race at Tramore on penultimate start

runners.name: Lincall
runners.spotlight: Weak form in maiden hurdles; pulled up at Limerick last time and a beaten favourite at Naas before that; hard to fancy unless better ground makes a radical difference.
runners.comment: Weak form in maiden hurdles, hard to fancy unless better ground makes a radical difference

runners.name: Warm In Gorey
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over hurdles; totally out of form over fences, pulled up on last three starts; blinkers tried now; main positive is that he has often run well at this venue, placed four times from seven visits, including twice last summer.
runners.comment: Out of form over fences, has run well at this venue, placed four times from seven visits

runners.name: Tasmanian Girl
runners.spotlight: Won a handicap hurdle at Thurles in March 2025; otherwise undistinguished form, including when tailed off in this race 12 months ago; should at least strip fitter as a result of a recent Flat run.
runners.comment: Won a handicap hurdle at Thurles in March 2025, the balance of her form is unappealing

runners.name: Rich Belief
runners.spotlight: Best hurdles form was in 2022, and he has done most of his racing on the Flat in recent years; second at Clonmel last September has been the only bright spot since a 2024 Chester win; no merit in his recent Navan run; may struggle to run up to an official rating of 117.
runners.comment: Best hurdles form was in 2022, may struggle to run up to an official rating of 117

runners.name: Colcannon
runners.spotlight: Grade 2 bumper winner at Leopardstown the season before last, and third in the Grade 1 Champion Bumper at Punchestown; got off the mark over hurdles on fourth start at Fairyhouse; raced too keenly and paid the price when fading late into fifth in a novice contest at the Punchestown festival; capable of being in the mix in this company.
runners.comment: Not the force he was in bumpers but sufficiently capable to be in the mix in this company

runners.name: Berto Ramirez
runners.spotlight: A 69-rated Flat maiden; struggled to make an impression in juvenile hurdles last winter and was a rank outsider in the Triumph; came good in a maiden at Down Royal early this month; won readily and left an impression that he can continue on the upgrade on summer ground.
runners.comment: Won readily at Down Royal, shaped as if he can continue on the upgrade on summer ground
runners.owner: D O'Mahony & D McHugh & D J Kelly & G Butler

runners.name: Arch Empire
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler; fair maiden on the Flat; useful form in small-field hurdle races in the first half of last season; should improve from a run in a handicap at the Punchestown festival; was one of the outsiders then, and made some late headway, plenty of horses behind him.
runners.comment: Should improve from a run in a handicap, made late headway, plenty of horses behind him

runners.name: Ifiwerearichman
runners.spotlight: Won a Punchestown novice chase in December 2024; ran only twice last season; now set for a first hurdles start since jumping badly at Listowel in September 2023; had previously produced a career-best when winning a handicap at the Galway festival; hard to be confident about his prospects.
runners.comment: Hard to be confident about his prospects after a spell over fences, rating dates from 2023

runners.name: Time Is A Thief
runners.spotlight: Went close against the useful Pure Steel on his only point start in 2024; had dropped away when falling at the last on hurdling debut at Cork last December; much more like it when making most to win at Tramore last month; fair chance now with his confidence boosted.
runners.comment: Made most to win at Tramore last month, fair chance now with his confidence boosted

runners.name: Moon Mission
runners.spotlight: Twice third in bumpers; third on handicap hurdle debut at Punchestown (2m5f, soft to heavy) in January; beaten a lot farther by the same winner when mid-field in a festival handicap at the same venue; suited by drop in trip and better ground when winning a novice event at Cork early this month; more on his plate here.
runners.comment: Suited by a drop in trip and decent ground when winning at Cork, this is harder
runners.owner: J T O'Connor & M P Gleeson & P M Fortune & A McGonigle

runners.name: Sageborough
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at this venue on debut last October; next two races were highly ambitious, in the Royal Bond and the Supreme Novices'; struggled in a conditions race at Cork last time; tongue-tie on now.
runners.comment: Won a maiden at this venue on debut last October, has been highly tried since

runners.name: Immutable
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner at Cork in March 2024; three-time Flat winner, best suited by soft and heavy ground; two moderate runs over hurdles last season; returned to form on the Flat with a good second over 1m4f at Cork last time; ground may be too lively here.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner in 2024, three-time Flat winner, best suited by soft/ heavy ground

runners.name: Sonny May
runners.spotlight: Career highlights when winning back-to-back handicap hurdles at Gowran and Punchestown in November 2024; a long way below his best in a light 2025 campaign; no encouragement from two handicap runs this month.
runners.comment: Career highlights were in 2024, no encouragement from two handicap runs this month

runners.name: Demanding Gerry
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in handicaps at Downpatrick and Kilbeggan last summer and again over 2m4f at Clonmel in October; slightly lame when beaten favourite later same month at Punchestown but definite player if reproducing those earlier efforts on her return here.
runners.comment: Absent since October but placed efforts last year give her definite claims

runners.name: Poppycon
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; standout run over hurdles when good second over C&D in March (soft) on handicap debut; beaten when faller two out over 2m4f at Tramore last month but big player back in trip here if handling quicker ground.
runners.comment: Faller at Tramore but previous C&D second gives him claims; reserve

runners.name: Broken Ice
runners.spotlight: Most recent of three wins came in 3m Perth handicap hurdle last summer; tailed off on comeback run at Newcastle and never counted over the banks at Punchestown festival early this month; trip on the short side reverting to hurdles.
runners.comment: Never counted over the banks at Punchestown; trip on the short side reverting to hurdles

runners.name: Granlahan
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; big odds and never involved in four maiden outings, bit more promising at Cork last time on good ground; market worth a check on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Bit more promising at Cork last time; market worth a check on handicap debut; reserve

runners.name: Walk On Annilog
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best handicap effort came at Punchestown 11 days ago on better ground, making much of running in finishing fourth of 18 over 2m; bit more needed but could go well.
runners.comment: Recent Punchestown fourth augurs well; bit more likely needed

runners.name: Grey Gardens
runners.spotlight: Returned from five month's absence for promising handicap debut early this month at Down Royal, none too fluent and short of room at crucial time when third of 20 over today's trip (good); contender, especially if jumping more fluently.
runners.comment: Promising recent Down Royal handicap debut; novicey jumper but definite player

runners.name: Backwithyourown
runners.spotlight: Some decent runs without winning in bumpers and maiden hurdles on mainly decent ground; headstrong sort didn't get home on handicap debut at Clonmel in October and given time off since; hood left off and probably best watched.
runners.comment: Headstrong sort didn't get home on handicap bow at Clonmel; best watched on return

runners.name: Single Edition
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner but 0-14 hurdling, joining current yard after C&D claiming fourth a year ago; didn't count on recent comeback run at Punchestown, entitled to come on for that.
runners.comment: Didn't count on recent comeback run at Punchestown, entitled to come on for that

runners.name: Kentucky Bluebird
runners.spotlight: Mildly promising run in Cork handicap in October (good), never counted on recent return at Sligo; entitled to come on for that and Donoghue back on.
runners.comment: Entitled to come on for recent comeback run and Donoghue back on

runners.name: Emesteraye
runners.spotlight: Some debut promise in C&D maiden in October but well held in better-class maidens twice following month on softer ground; opening handicap mark looks on the high side but yard going well so worth watching for market support.
runners.comment: Debut promise in C&D maiden in October but well held afterwards; opening mark looks high

runners.name: Kome Dans Un Reve
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 6yo entering handicap company from a respected source; market watch advised.
runners.comment: Unexposed 6yo entering handicap company from a respected source; market watch advised

runners.name: Ray Gun
runners.spotlight: Won at a modest level on AW in Britain; weak maiden hurdle form and always behind for his first handicap hurdle at Clonmel when pulled up; only a 4yo so time on his side.
runners.comment: Always behind on handicap debut at Clonmel when pulled up; has time on his side though

runners.name: Kilt
runners.spotlight: Useful Flat form as 3yo but yet to make an impact hurdling, tailed off at Cork last month.
runners.comment: Useful Flat form as 3yo but yet to make an impact hurdling, tailed off at Cork last month

runners.name: Lunar Landscape
runners.spotlight: Placed on the Flat at a modest level; trio of maiden hurdle runs (on heavy ground) have offered little but potential improver on better ground for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Potential improver on better ground for handicap debut

runners.name: Del Boys Diva
runners.spotlight: First reserve; no impact in a trio of maidens over the winter; market best guide now on handicap debut.
runners.comment: No impact in maidens over the winter, market best guide on handicap debut; reserve

runners.name: Syosset
runners.spotlight: Ended a poor run of form with a shock Dundalk AW win in November, okay comeback run there in March; 0-5 hurdling, last time when tailed off in Downpatrick handicap in September.
runners.comment: AW winner but not as good a hurdler so far

runners.name: Boltlander
runners.spotlight: Moderate maiden form and pulled up on handicap debut at Cork in November, his final start for Gordon Elliott; makes stable debut now.
runners.comment: Pulled up on handicap debut at Cork in November, his final start for Gordon Elliott

runners.name: Katherine
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner at 2m on decent ground but not since October 2024; no show on both recent outings but can't be ruled out down in grade here.
runners.comment: No show on both recent outings but can't be ruled out down in grade here

runners.name: Whiskey Serenade
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner and placed in modest maiden hurdle at Tramore (2m5f, good) last summer; couple of spins over fences didn't work out and well held twice over hurdles of late; blinkers now added to tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Well held twice over hurdles of late; blinkers now added to tongue-tie

runners.name: Redcliff Glen
runners.spotlight: Limerick handicap hurdle winner in October 2024 off 94; little of note in his form since then however, recent Punchestown run a bit more encouraging; more needed.
runners.comment: Recent Punchestown run a bit more encouraging; more needed

runners.name: Deise Crystal
runners.spotlight: Still novicey in jumping when decent C&D second 11 days ago (good-to-yielding); this looks more competitive but still definite claims.
runners.comment: Recent C&D second; this looks more competitive but still definite claims

runners.name: Topothelane
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner is now 0-8 hurdling, recent modest Tramore third (2m6f, yielding) mildly encouraging; back in trip here.
runners.comment: Recent modest Tramore third (2m6f, yielding) mildly encouraging; back in trip here

runners.name: Bryson
runners.spotlight: Took part in some decent maidens; brought down on handicap debut in December and following day shaped nicely when staying on late in Naas amateur handicap after being short of room; potential for more, especially back on better ground.
runners.comment: Naas run back in December encouraging; potential for more now back on better ground
runners.owner: Nigel O'Hare & Mark Devlin & Philip Smith

runners.name: Big Dee
runners.spotlight: Useful bumper form in 2023, winning at Down Royal; hurdling career so far hasn't matched up, Limerick handicap third at Christmas (heavy) his best effort; pulled a shoe and suffered an overreach when pulled up in similar (but weaker) event in February; tongue-tie added now; can't rule out but may want it softer.
runners.comment: Excuses last time; tongue-tie added now; can't rule out but may want it softer

runners.name: Euro Maker
runners.spotlight: Promising second handicap attempt when chasing home a 6l winner in 20-runner 2m Cork contest last month, having made much of the running; just 2lb higher and big run expected.
runners.comment: Front-running Cork second just 2lb higher and big run expected

runners.name: Thehardandthewild
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; twice placed in points; struggling in handicap hurdles over the winter and makes limited appeal.
runners.comment: Struggled in handicap hurdles last winter and of limited appeal; reserve

runners.name: Brave Josee
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly never landed a blow on recent handicap debut at Sligo but did make late headway; tongue-tied now added and no surprise if she had more to offer here.
runners.comment: Late headway on recent handicap bow;  tongue-tied added and likely more to offer

runners.name: Bella Union
runners.spotlight: Veteran finally made the breakthrough in 2m1 Bellewstown handicap in April and duly followed up at Down Royal early this month off 5lb higher under today's rider, produced late for cosy success; hit with another 8lb rise but in the form of his life and ground remains in his favour.
runners.comment: Hat-trick seeking 10yo up 8lb for latest but ground remains in his favour

runners.name: Western Opera
runners.spotlight: Standout run in handicaps came when second in 2m6f Ballinrobe contest last summer (good); absent since August and debuts for new yard off what appears an inadequate trip.
runners.comment: Absent since August and debuts for new yard off what appears an inadequate trip

runners.name: Cinnabar Moth
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; glimpse of ability on Cork bumper debut last year but nothing so far in maiden hurdles; market best guide entering handicap company.
runners.comment: Nothing so far in maidens, market best guide entering handicap company; reserve

runners.name: Monty's Way
runners.spotlight: Dual-Flat winner is 1-15 over timber, the win coming off 6lb higher at Ballinrobe two years ago; well below that level last autumn and probably best watched on seasonal return.
runners.comment: Sole win came two years ago off 6lb higher; lacks a recent run so best watched

runners.name: Backinabit
runners.spotlight: First reserve; ran well at Roscommon in September after layoff, well held twice afterwards for Ross O'Sullivan; first start for Denis Hogan.
runners.comment: Absent since December; best watched on first start for Denis Hogan; reserve

runners.name: Fine Print
runners.spotlight: AW winner went close at Wolverhampton 16 days ago; 0-4 hurdling in the space of as many weeks last summer but clearly in good heart and could go well.
runners.comment: Recent AW second; unexposed hurdling, clearly in good heart and could go well

runners.name: My Mate Tony
runners.spotlight: Mildly encouraging form as a juvenile hurdler but handicap debut run at Roscommon in September was poor; probably best watched on return.
runners.comment: Mildly encouraging juvenile form but handicap debut at Roscommon in September poor

runners.name: Karlynn
runners.spotlight: Some merit in two runs at Clonmel; better ground brought about improvement when second to an odds-on chance at Cork; fair chance getting weight from older rivals.
runners.comment: Better ground brought about improvement when second at Cork, fair chance at these weights

runners.name: Tippawan Haa
runners.spotlight: Showed winning potential when second at Cork last August (2m3f, good); reported to have burst a blood vessel when well beaten at Ballinrobe later that month; may have needed last month's run at Bellewstown after a lengthy break.
runners.comment: Best effort when second at Cork last August, may have needed last month's Bellewstown run

runners.name: Casual Water
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to the top-class Altior and several other smart performers; tailed off over 3m in testing conditions at Limerick on debut; shorter trip (2m2f) and better ground failed to improve him at Sligo; however, only three days later, showed promise with a third placing at Kilbeggan.
runners.comment: Half-brother to Altior, weak form before showing promise with a third placing at Kilbeggan

runners.name: Forever And A Day
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in her first two maiden hurdles and in a bumper; last month's Down Royal second was a big step in the right direction; more needed now
runners.comment: Last month's Down Royal second was a big step in the right direction, more needed now

runners.name: Lumiere Du Large
runners.spotlight: Flat winner and second over hurdles in France; out of her depth in Grade 1s at huge prices in early 2025 and regressed alarmingly in three runs last season before leaving Willie Mullins; some encouragement can be taken from her recent second at Sligo on first start for Gordon Elliott; however, this appears to be a deeper race.
runners.comment: Some encouragement can be taken from her recent second at Sligo, this race looks tougher

runners.name: Grandy Lane
runners.spotlight: Just denied in a 2m4f maiden hurdle at Sligo last May when with Ian Donoghue; again went close on stable debut in a Clonmel bumper last November (soft); failed to build on that when sent back over hurdles; more lively effort last time, staying on when falling at the last at Bellewstown.
runners.comment: Not the most dependable type, staying on when falling at the last at Bellewstown
runners.owner: Darren Cahill/JRM Racing Syndicate/Mrs J P Duffy

runners.name: Happy Me
runners.spotlight: Placed in four of his 14 starts over hurdles; well exposed now; unlikely to make the breakthrough in this company.
runners.comment: Placed in four of his 14 starts over hurdles; unlikely to make the breakthrough here

runners.name: Tiger Twins
runners.spotlight: Poor bumper form but has shown more aptitude for hurdling; not beaten far in fifth at Killarney last time; can be fancied to uphold form with the more exposed Happy Me.
runners.comment: Poor bumper form but has shown more aptitude for hurdling, fair fifth at Killarney

runners.name: Youneedabitofluck
runners.spotlight: Left previous modest efforts behind when staying on to finish 4l second behind a Willie Mullins-trained favourite at Tramore (2m, soft) last November; backed up that promising effort with a third placing in a Cork novice event in January (yielding-to-soft); could feature if showing similar form on better ground after a good break.
runners.comment: Improved form before a break, could feature if showing similar form on better ground

runners.name: Jury Foreman
runners.spotlight: Beaten similar distances in three maidens and on handicap debut at Leopardstown; fading when unseating at the last at Bellewstown last time.
runners.comment: Beaten similar distances in three maidens, fading when unseating at the last last time

runners.name: Jonny Carrol
runners.spotlight: Two minor placed efforts for Gordon Elliott; struggled in his first handicap at Cork last December; little appeal on first run for this local stable.
runners.comment: Two minor placed efforts for Gordon Elliott, little appeal on first run for this  stable

runners.name: Maybe Someday
runners.spotlight: Managed a fair run in a bumper at Tramore; weak form in maiden hurdles; headgear tried now
runners.comment: Managed a fair run in a bumper at Tramore, weak form in maiden hurdles,  headgear tried

runners.name: Clone Cross
runners.spotlight: A maiden in points; very poor form in four maiden hurdles.
runners.comment: A maiden in points very poor form in four maiden hurdles

runners.name: Old Saxony
runners.spotlight: Won at the eighth and final attempt on the Flat for David Simcock in a 1m6f Southwell AW handicap in October 2024; limited impact in three races over hurdles; better ground may bring about improvement, but not suited by the race conditions.
runners.comment: Better ground may bring about improvement but not suited by the race conditions

runners.name: Dani Donadoni
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner over 2m3f; off the mark over hurdles over the same course and trip in March at the fourth attempt, winning by a handsome margin at short odds; unseated at the first on handicap debut at the Punchestown festival; hard to be sure if he is worthy of an official rating of 130 that gives him an excellent chance here in theory.
runners.comment: Hard to be sure if he is worthy of a 130 rating that gives him a strong theoretical chance

runners.name: Lemmy Caution
runners.spotlight: Cost 250,000GBP after 8l debut point win at Monksgrange last year; bumper form was ordinary; may have turned a corner when making a winning debut over hurdles in a 3m maiden at Punchestown a fortnight ago; blinkered for the first time.
runners.comment: Bumper form was ordinary, may have turned a corner with Punchestown win on hurdles debut

runners.name: Rocky's Howya
runners.spotlight: Won three consecutive races in February/March on heavy, two claiming hurdles and a handicap chase. reported blowing hard after being pulled up in a novice hurdle at this venue last month; back to form in first-time blinkers when winning a claiming hurdle at Clonmel 13 days ago; faces a stiff task at these weights.
runners.comment: Won a claiming hurdle at Clonmel 13 days ago, faces a stiff task at these weights.

runners.name: Park Of Kings
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner; failed to adapt to chasing in six starts last season, including in novice handicaps on his last three outings; best hurdles form at 2m-2m4f, though ran with credit when fourth in a strong 3m handicap at Aintree last year.
runners.comment: Failed to adapt to chasing in six starts last season, probably best watched here

runners.name: Kiltealy Park
runners.spotlight: Winner of two of her nine starts over fences since her last hurdles run almost two years ago; recorded her two hurdle wins back-to-back over 2m4f at this venue in April/May 2024; below best over fences since a fair run at last year's Galway festival.
runners.comment: Won back-to-back hurdle races over 2m4f at this venue in April/May 2024, chasing lately

runners.name: Sean Mc
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; breakthrough win came here last month over 2m4f (good-to-yielding); didn't count over 3m1f at Kilbeggan a fortnight later however so stamina concerns persist.
runners.comment: 2m4f winner here last month but 3m run since inconclusive; reserve

runners.name: Riyami
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner whose hurdles win came in 2m Kilbeggan handicap nearly two years ago (good); recent Flat comeback run should have him fitter although unproven over this far.
runners.comment: Sole hurdles win nearly two years ago; fitter for recent Flat run but unproven at 3m

runners.name: Hitthehayson
runners.spotlight: Winner of handicap hurdles last term at Roscommon and Downpatrick before recording wins over fences at Fairyhouse and Haydock; pulled up over fences on latest but previous Down Royal hurdle second gives him claims; tongue-tie added to usual cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Fine form last term hurdling/chasing; remains on a competitive mark

runners.name: Lecale's Diamond
runners.spotlight: Maiden has been mixing hurdling and chasing, well-backed on a few occasions, including recent comeback at Downpatrick when tried tongue-tied, only tiring from the last when fair fourth (2m6f hurdle, good); tries a new trip.
runners.comment: Expensive to follow but recent comeback run a solid one; contender if staying longer trip

runners.name: Lily Milan
