runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner showed signs of ability in bumpers but her maiden hurdle runs below that, most recently seemingly not getting home in 2m3f Cork mares' maiden last month; back up in trip here for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Hurdles form hasn't so far matched up to bumper form; upped in trip for handicap debut

runners.name: Orinoco Walk
runners.spotlight: Didn't really land a blow on recent handicap debut over this trip at Cork; hard to see her making the improvement needed.
runners.comment: Didn't really land a blow on recent handicap debut over this trip at Cork; hard to fancy

runners.name: Annie Button
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win when upped to 3m1f at Cork earlier this month with cheekpieces fitted, appreciating better ground when rallying late on after being headed by favourite; 6lb higher but conditions again to suit and could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Recent Cork winner on good ground; 6lb higher but conditions again to suit so shortlisted

runners.name: Oogum Boogum
runners.spotlight: Finally off the mark at 19th attempt over hurdles when landing 3m Punchestown handicap a fortnight ago (good), making all in beating Ballinaboola Gold 1.5l (pair clear); 6lb rise for that and meets that rival on 3lb worse terms.
runners.comment: Recent all-the-way Punchestown defeat of Ballinaboola Gold over this trip; 3lb worse off

runners.name: The Cobblers Wife
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; okay form at best in handicaps, lost action when pulled up at Punchestown last time.
runners.comment: Okay form in handicaps, lost action when pulled up at Punchestown last time; reserve

runners.name: Ballinaboola Gold
runners.spotlight: Maiden took a nice step forward over this trip at Punchestown a fortnight ago, well clear of the remainder when outstayed by all-the-way winner Oogum Boogum (good); 3lb better off with that rival now so has her chance.
runners.comment: Outstayed by front-running Oogum Boogum over this trip at Punchestown; 3lb better off

runners.name: Queen Kulina
runners.spotlight: First reserve; Galway fourth over this trip last October (yielding) gives her claims but recent Punchestown fall (behind Oogum Boogum) not encouraging.
runners.comment: Has form at 3m but recent Punchestown fall (behind Oogum Boogum) not encouraging; reserve

runners.name: Appian Way
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been at shorter trips but has run well over 3m; back down to career-low mark now on recent modest efforts.
runners.comment: Wins over shorter; back down to career-low mark now on recent modest efforts

runners.name: Knocknagoran Lady
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best efforts have come on good ground, including over this trip at Down Royal last month in this headgear combination when third; place claims again.
runners.comment: Recent Down Royal third over this trip gives her place claims again

runners.name: True Harmony
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner on this sort of ground but not too much of note so far hurdling, 30l off Oogum Boogum at Punchestown recently; plenty to find.
runners.comment: 30l off Oogum Boogum at Punchestown recently; plenty to find

runners.name: Dearg Mor
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on handicap debut over 2m3f at Punchestown in January (heavy); first run on this sort of ground and cheekpieces added; big improvement required.
runners.comment: First run on this sort of ground and cheekpieces added; big improvement required

runners.name: Jack Inthe Jukebox
runners.spotlight: Modest form in point-to-points and maiden hurdles; pulled up after never counting on last month's handicap debut at Down Royal; headgear combination needs to bring serious improvement.
runners.comment: Pulled up after never counting on last month's handicap debut; headgear tried here

runners.name: Jetstream Jeff
runners.spotlight: Hardly beaten a rival in four maidens, impossible to make a case for on handicap debut unless quicker ground brings improvement.
runners.comment: Impossible to make a case for on handicap debut unless quicker ground brings improvement

races.race_name: Wexford Racing Again 30th June INH Flat Race

runners.name: Kisuton Enki
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to several French winner, notably high-class hurdler/chaser up to 3m4f Yala Enki; returned from lengthy absence to finish decent second in 2m6f Limerick mares' maiden hurdle in April (soft) and earlier this month chased all-the-way winner for much of the way in 2m2f Downpatrick mares bumper on good ground; return to 2m4f to suit and should go well.
runners.comment: Downpatrick second back up in trip and should go well

runners.name: Weaponized
runners.spotlight: Second foal of bumper-winning sister to 2m5f/3m1f chase winner In Great Form, half-sister to 2m4f/2m5f chase winner Moodofthemoment; hooded, promising debut early this month when second, leading home turn until outpaced by a Flat-bred sort in 2m2f Down Royal bumper (good); longer trip here to suit and major player.
runners.comment: Recent Down Royal second on debut; longer trip here to suit and major player

runners.name: Square Jasmin
runners.spotlight: Hunter's Light gelding; E17,000 Y, E23,000 3yo; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Manipura (German 1m Listed) and Lance Les Des (2m hurdle); promising recent debut in Cork 4yo maiden hurdle, staying on to dead-heat for fourth behind a smart sort (2m2f, good); noteworthy jockey booking now switched to bumper.
runners.comment: Recent Cork maiden hurdle fourth; noteworthy jockey booking now switched to bumper

runners.name: He's My Hero
runners.spotlight: Long-absent 8yo showed plenty of ability when second (to Croke Park) in a Dromahane point-to-point and in bumpers in early 2023 when second in good Naas contest and beaten 4l in steadily-run Punchestown event (yielding); major player if able to reproduce that level of form here after more than three years off the track.
runners.comment: Smart form in early 2023 but absent over three years

runners.name: Corway
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; Vadamos 4yo is fifth foal of a maiden sister to French 1m2f winner Melbourne Shuffle, half-sister to Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner Pichola Dance, family of 2yo Group 1 winners Listen and Sequoyah; tongue-tied for debut and worth a market check.
runners.comment: 4yo tongue-tied for debut and worth a market check; reserve

runners.name: Moundofthehostages
runners.spotlight: Diamond Boy 5yo is fourth foal of an unraced sister to winners Eightybarackstreet (useful bumper and 2m-2m1f hurdle/chase) and Third Act (bumper/2m hurdle), half-sister to useful 2m-3m hurdle/chase winner Canaly; headgear combination for debut.
runners.comment: Out of a sister to a couple of bumper winners; headgear combination for debut

runners.name: Rumi Rock
runners.spotlight: Jukebox Jury 5yo cost 12,000euros as a 3yo; half-brother to winners Broomfield Hall (bumper and 2m hurdle/chase) and Ernie Bilko (2m6f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to useful bumper and 2m3f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Keepitsecret; point-to-point form modest, best watched on rules debut.
runners.comment: Point-to-point form modest, best watched on rules debut

runners.name: Union Boy
runners.spotlight: Workforce gelding; dam point/2m5f hurdle winner, out of half-sister to bumper winning dam of useful 2m/2m4f hurdle/chase winner Cruising Katie; market best guide on debut.
runners.comment: Newcomer for whom market best guide on debut

runners.name: Siege Of Carthage
runners.spotlight: Getaway 6yo whose dam was a 2m4f hurdle winner, closely related to high-class staying chaser Conflated; ran once in a point-to-point, returning after lengthy absence when chasing home an easy winner in a bumper at Gowran in March (2m2f, heavy); more required.
runners.comment: Remote Gowran second steps up in trip; more required

runners.name: Away To Paris
runners.spotlight: Way To Paris an 11,000euros 3yo; second foal; dam, by Robin Des Champs, unraced half-sister to useful winners Not Many Left, Coverholder and Angus Milan; okay recent Clonmel debut run, good bit more required to feature here.
runners.comment: Okay recent Clonmel debut run, good bit more required to feature here

runners.name: Double Order
runners.spotlight: Order Of St George mare well held on debut (too keen) in Cork bumper a year ago for Pat Turley, reappearing for current yard early this month at Downpatrick when never sighted in first-time hood; probably best watched for now.
runners.comment: No show on recent Downpatrick comeback run; may need more time

runners.name: Crios Cu Chulainn
runners.spotlight: Idaho mare; dam fourth in bumper, half-sister to Champion Hurdle winner Faugheen; tailed off on bumper debut at Punchestown a fortnight ago, can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Tailed off on bumper debut at Punchestown a fortnight ago, can only be watched for now

runners.name: Bolta Tintreach
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; Beat Hollow 7yo cost 3,300euros as a yearling; half-brother to 2m4f/2m7f hurdle winner Vereina; dam point/2m7f chase winner, half-sister to 2m-2m4f hurdle/useful chase winner Nadiya De La Vega; best watched on belated debut.
runners.comment: Cheaply bought as a yearling, 7yo best watched on belated debut; reserve

runners.name: Northernlightsky
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; nothing in point-to-points and three runs in bumpers don't suggest she'll feature here.
runners.comment: Three runs in bumpers don't suggest she'll feature here; reserve

runners.name: Prince Malinas
runners.spotlight: Bellewstown effort a step up on Limerick debut but still lots more likely required here.
runners.comment: Bellewstown effort a step up on Limerick debut but still lots more likely required here

runners.name: The Boysofairhill
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to bumper/hurdles winner Long Gone; had shown little before winning a maiden point-to-point at Dromahane in April; bit headstrong in first-time cheekpieces on rules debut in 2m1f bumper here early this month, weakening before home turn; upped in trip now.
runners.comment: Headstrong in first-time cheekpieces on rules debut in 2m1f bumper here early this month

runners.name: Megansue
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Powerstown Park (useful 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase), Grageelagh Girl (bumper/2m3f hurdle), Tom Cody (2m3f-3m chase) and Ryan's Rocket (useful 2m hurdle/chase); nothing of note in point-to-points.
runners.comment: Related to some decent types but nothing of note in point-to-points

races.race_name: Unibet More Extra Place Races Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: U S S Constitution
runners.spotlight: Juvenile winner at Ascot for this yard (7f) before gelded and sent to John Size in Hong Kong; disappointingly failed to win there but, given he'd ran to an RPR of 89 in Britain previously, it was no surprise to see him score off BHA mark of 55 on his return here in February (7f); sharper track when kept to 7f then a slow pace when returned to 1m scuppered him since; major claims from handy draw.
runners.comment: Can prove himself well handicapped if getting good pace to aim at

runners.name: Penfolds Grange
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back seconds over C&D and shaped as if still in form when sixth to Joseph over 2f further at Lingfield last time; not out of this; trainer/owner also represented by Forca Timao.
runners.comment: Back-to-back seconds over C&D and shaped as if still in form last time

runners.name: Goldie Trickett
runners.spotlight: Won't be justifying her seven-figure yearling price but has joined the yard who trained her winning sisters (including Saffron Beach, who struck twice at the top level) and she showed enough at Southwell last month to suggest a win or two is achievable; didn't see 1m2f out at Yarmouth last week; due 2lb drop; first-time cheekpieces replace hood.
runners.comment: Showed enough on yard debut to suggest a win or two is achievable; stretched latest

runners.name: Tex
runners.spotlight: Very pleasing return from 275-day break at Leicester this month; lacked a change of gear at Newmarket (also 7f) subsequently and returning to 1m looks in his favour; draw hasn't been kind, though.
runners.comment: Returning to 1m looks in his favour but draw hasn't been kind

runners.name: Joseph
runners.spotlight: Two wins (one for this jockey) and a second since asked to go beyond an extended 1m; has form at shorter but wide draw could put him on the back foot early; one of three runners for John Butler.
runners.comment: In good form over further of late; wide draw could put him on the back foot early

runners.name: Valentine Boy
runners.spotlight: Managed third in a C&D 0-80 last month and duly did the business in 0-75 company, also over track and trip, 23 days ago; this is even easier, although he's up 4lb, and Jack Dace can keep the ride in this apprentice contest; headgear (hood) goes on, presumably in attempt to settle him.
runners.comment: Has plenty going for him and could easily be 3-5 over C&D after this

runners.name: Gennadius
runners.spotlight: Put his experience to good use at Chelmsford in January and nearly rallied to win at Lingfield the following month (both 1m); not as potent in three runs since and will turn up here having been gelded; stall 12 tricky given he likes to race prominently.
runners.comment: Could do with gelding operation having the desired effect and is drawn wide

runners.name: Forca Timao
runners.spotlight: Outsider of four when belatedly doubling his tally at Southwell last April (1m3f); hit the frame last two starts (1m2f) but not sure dropping back to 1m will see him play a bigger role this evening; Penfolds Grange looks connections' best hope.
runners.comment: Hit the frame last two starts (1m2f) but not sure dropping back to 1m is the answer

runners.name: Shamacid
runners.spotlight: 0-12 but runner-up five times and went down by a nose and a neck following a gelding operation here two starts ago (form boosted since); this kind of trip has stretched him in the past, though; jockey Katrine Holm seeks first winner.
runners.comment: Definite form claims but this kind of trip has stretched him in the past

runners.name: Darvel
runners.spotlight: Normally starts slowly; goes well fresh so would like to have seen more on reappearance at Bath and didn't fare much better there four weeks ago; fourth is the best he's managed on AW; passed over.
runners.comment: Fourth is the best he's managed on AW; passed over

runners.name: Kit Gabriel
runners.spotlight: Knocking on the door with increasing vigour and went down by just half-a-length in a 0-80 here three weeks ago (7f); no issue with returning to further (has won over 8.6f) and a 2lb rise is justified; claims.
runners.comment: Knocking on the door with increasing vigour; claims

runners.name: Rhythm N Rock
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in 2022 and narrowly failed to land a gamble off 1lb lower under these conditions just before Christmas; not as effective since but is worth a market check, particularly versus John Butler's other two runners.
runners.comment: Not the easiest to predict but is worth a market check

runners.name: Prince Rasam
runners.spotlight: Promise to begin with for former and current yard; might find things happening too quickly running over a trip this short for the first time; back from a break.
runners.comment: Might find things happening too quickly running over a trip this short for the first time

runners.name: Gunfighter
runners.spotlight: Dual winner for Hugo Palmer (6f/7f); third the best he managed for Robert Eddery; failed to see out 1m in two previous attempts so looks best watched on debut for John Butler, who runs two others, back from 292 days off.
runners.comment: Failed to see out 1m in two previous attempts so looks best watched on stable debut/return

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Phalanx Nation
runners.spotlight: February foal; £82,000 yearling by Sioux Nation; held in third when odds-on for Nottingham debut (6f, good) last month but she got pestered for the lead and was beaten less than 2l in the end; that was a promising start and she should improve for the experience; key player.
runners.comment: Held when odds-on at Nottingham but that was a promising debut; key player

runners.name: Moonrise
runners.spotlight: April foal; Churchill half-sister to Qatari 6f/7f winner Silca Blue (including 2yo); dam 7f/1m winner (RPR 80), half-sister to winners Concorde (1m/1m2f) and Key Bid (1m2f-1m5f), out of Group-placed 6f 2yo winner; Oisin Murphy is booked on debut and she needs a close look.
runners.comment: Churchill filly; Oisin Murphy is booked on debut and she needs a close look

runners.name: Wise And Wonderful
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to winners Gold Cup Day (1m4f; RPR 86) and Ingleton (Omani 6f); dam 1m4f winner (92), closely related to 1m2f-2m4f Group 1 winner Fame And Glory and to dam of 1m-1m2f Group 1 winner Legatissimo; one of two newcomers for the yard and market should guide.
runners.comment: Out of a 1m4f winner; one of two newcomers for the yard and market should guide

runners.name: Esha'a
runners.spotlight: £190,000 yearling; third foal; dam 7f/1m winner (including Swedish Listed), half-sister to two French winners out of 1m winner; yard 18% with 2yos last year and she's an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: £190,000 yearling; yard 18% with 2yos last year and she's an interesting newcomer

runners.name: Alice Rocks
runners.spotlight: 13,000gns foal, £22,000 yearling; sister to 7f 2yo winner Who Is Alice (RPR 67), half-sister to winners Over Spiced (multiple 5f including 2yo; 75), Lady Magda (Belgian 10.5f-13.5f) and Vive La Danse (Belgian 10.5f-13.5f); dam 6f/7f winner (including 2yo; 94); yard 2-50 with 2yos in recent years and she could be one for later on.
runners.comment: £22,000 yearling; yard 2-50 with 2yos in recent years and she could be one for later on
runners.owner: Mps Racing And Jjg Racing Partnership

runners.name: Iris Olivia
runners.spotlight: 16-1 and she weakened quickly when a remote seventh of eight on Nottingham debut (6f, good) last month.
runners.comment: Weakened quickly when a remote seventh of eight on Nottingham debut (6f, good) last month

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Erudition
runners.spotlight: Two promising 7f runs on turf this spring, keeping on well into third at Leicester on debut and pressing a useful previous winner at Newbury 12 days ago; may appreciate an extra furlong in due course but has shown enough to think he can win over today's trip at this level.
runners.comment: Two promising 7f runs on turf; good form claims now switched to the AW

runners.name: Makerstoun
runners.spotlight: Minor promise on Newmarket debut and stepped up plenty when looking the likely winner over C&D  last month, only to get reeled in late by a useful one who looks destined for better things; a likely player.
runners.comment: Useful 2nd over C&D in April, reeled in late by useful rival; likely player

runners.name: Slalom
runners.spotlight: 130,000euros yearling; by No Nay Never out of mare with several smart siblings, including multiple Group winner Echo Of Light; mixed messages from pedigree regarding trip but the market should be helpful.
runners.comment: 130,000euros yearling; dam had several smart siblings; market should be helpful

runners.name: Mmmbop
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure prices for two starts over 1m, sticking well to the task when a never-nearer third on this track in April; the drop to 7f raises questions but she's worth keeping an eye on with a view to handicaps later.
runners.comment: Kept on well when taking a late 3rd over 1m here latest; drop to 7f not an obvious plus

runners.name: Distant Moon
runners.spotlight: Well-connected colt; easy to back for recent Leicester debut (7f; 13-2) where he raced freely/erratically and had no answer to a more experienced rival in the closing stages; the bare form puts him in the picture and he should know more this time.
runners.comment: Raced freely/erratically when 2nd on turf debut (7f); should know more this time

runners.name: Jean's Boy
runners.spotlight: 40-1 for last week's Lingfield debut over 6f where he ran green and was beaten a long way in last of six.
runners.comment: 40-1 and finished remote on Lingfield debut over 6f last week

runners.name: Khaleejy
runners.spotlight: Useful pedigree for middle distances but he had no trouble with going the gallop over 7f on Newcastle AW in April on belated debut, sealing the outcome quite convincingly under Kyle McHugh; has potential; bold bid.
runners.comment: Travelled strongly before winning 7f debut on Newcastle AW on belated debut; has potential

runners.name: Don Pablo Colina
runners.spotlight: Dam won at up to 2m; 125-1 shot for recent 1m debut on Lingfield AW where he lost his position in the closing stages; major query about the drop to 7f and handicaps look the next step.
runners.comment: 125-1 for recent 1m debut on AW and dropped out; trip query at 7f; handicaps soon

runners.name: Siddal
runners.spotlight: Well held in the end on her Newbury debut (about 6f; 16-1) last October but she looked to have ability and should do better as a 3yo; however, she needs to have come on a ton to win this after a layoff.
runners.comment: Well beaten on Newbury debut but did show signs of ability; likely to need this

runners.name: Sahm Naif
runners.spotlight: Big prices and soundly beaten in 7f turf maiden and an AW novice (1m) for Michael Keady as 2yo; gelded since; handicaps will be more suitable.
runners.comment: Big prices and soundly beaten in 7f and 1m events as 2yo; best watched for new yard

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Masked Warrior
runners.spotlight: Two promising runs as a juvenile, at 7f and about 1m, but they were on good to soft and soft ground and he may have been feeling the good to firm surface when hanging under pressure on his Leicester return; should be capable of much better; cheekpieces fitted.
runners.comment: Two promising runs as 2yo (good to soft/soft); hung on good to firm latest; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Storming Point
runners.spotlight: Dam was Group and Listed-placed at up to 1m1f in France; 13-2 for Newbury debut (extended 6f, soft) in October where he only reluctantly gave up his lead (to one who was close up in a Group 3 this spring) before coming back for more late on; has very different conditions today.
runners.comment: Runner-up at about 6f (soft) at Newbury in sole 2yo start and that form reads well

runners.name: Conspiracist
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; Dark Angel half-brother to winners Sajir (5.5f-7f including 2yo/AW/Group 1; RPR 122) and First Kingdom (1m including AW; 92); dam Group-placed 6f AW 2yo winner (91); the draw could have been kinder but the market can guide.
runners.comment: Siblings include a Group 1 winner; draw could have been kinder but market can guide

runners.name: Back At One
runners.spotlight: 160,000euros yearling; third foal; by Ten Sovereigns; gelded half-brother to 1m4f AW winner Palmezzano (RPR 79); dam unraced half-sister to 5.5f Listed winner Iffranesia; yard has a strong strike-rate in AW maiden and novice events in recent years so the betting needs watching.
runners.comment: 160,000euros yearling; yard has strong strike-rate in AW maidens/novices; check market

runners.name: Wintercast
runners.spotlight: Ran a long way below market expectations when beaten over 14l at Doncaster (6f; favourite) in April and comes here with plenty to prove over an extra furlong with a hood fitted.
runners.comment: Favourite, beaten over 14l on 6f debut in April; enough to prove upped to 7f; now hooded

runners.name: Olympic Anthem
runners.spotlight: Third foal; Victor Ludorum half-brother to 6f/7f AW/dirt winner Pen Portrait (including 2yo; RPR 90); dam 1m/1m2f winner (including AW; 93), half-sister to 7f-1m2f Group 1 winner Rio De La Plata; plenty to like on paper but the fitting of a hood suggests he may want to rush early.
runners.comment: Dam half-sister to multiple Group 1 winner; plenty to like on paper except the hood

runners.name: Foxy Night
runners.spotlight: Third foal; Night Of Thunder half-sister to 8.4f winner Drink Dry (RPR 81); unraced dam's winning siblings include 1m2f/10.5f Group 1 winner Fascinating Rock and two useful 1m4f winners; might need longer trips but yard is in good form and she's worth a market check.
runners.comment: Pedigree suggests longer trips might suit but yard in good form so worth a market check

runners.name: He's Perfect
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros foal, 65,000euros breeze-up 2yo; sixth foal; by Sioux Nation; winning siblings include  5f/6f winner Keep You Warm (including 2yo/HK; RPR 99) and Dallas Girl (German 1m1f); dam placed 1m1f in France; yard's runners can go well on debut so not ruled out.
runners.comment: 65,000euros breeze-up 2yo; yard's runners can go well on debut so not ruled out

runners.name: Tyrant Gg
runners.spotlight: Just 11-4 for his recent debut over 6f on this track where he kept on nicely enough into fourth of 11; should improve for the extra furlong and possibly the tongue-tie but others look more likely.
runners.comment: 11-4 for recent 6f debut here when a fair 4th; should improve but needs plenty more

runners.name: Decalogue
runners.spotlight: Never near the leaders in two 7f events, over C&D and on dirt at Meydan in the autumn; cheekpieces on latest, now discarded; likely other days.
runners.comment: Never near leaders over C&D and on dirt at Meydan in the autumn; likely other days

runners.name: Water To Wine
runners.spotlight: Strong in the betting on debut at Newbury (1m3f) and backers always looked like collecting; carries a 7lb penalty here but is seemingly held in some regard given his next outing was meant to be in the Chester Vase (scratched due to having a temperature) and an entry in the Arc (he's around 50-1) has been made.
runners.comment: Looks up to defying a 7lb penalty given the impression he made on debut; holds Arc entry

runners.name: Barrister
runners.spotlight: Fair fourth on debut here in October (7f) and was up against some very nice types when seventh at Newbury last month (1m2f; achieved similar amount); will be of greater interest once handicapping.
runners.comment: First two runs not devoid of promise but will be of greater interest once handicapping

runners.name: Take A Chill Pill
runners.spotlight: Finished fifth on debut at Newmarket and still looked in need of the experience when filling the same position, and recording the same RPR, at Chester 19 days ago (upped from 1m to 12.2f); headgear (cheekpieces) could help to focus him but he's another who will likely come into his own once handicapping.
runners.comment: Finished fifth in first two starts; will likely come into his own once handicapping

runners.name: White Storm
runners.spotlight: 170,000gns yearling; third foal; brother to two winners, including White Birch (7f-10.5f, including AW/Group 1; RPR 125); dam 6f/7f AW winner (100); already gelded; makes some appeal on paper and training partnership has 21% strike-rate in AW maidens/novices in the last five years.
runners.comment: 170,000gns yearling; brother to White Birch; newcomer to note

runners.name: Tripoli Flyer
runners.spotlight: Bagged a bumper and three hurdles, including Grade 2 Dovecote last February; acquitted himself well in two big spring festivals of late; should have a future on the Flat but will likely get a mark after this third start; no headgear.
runners.comment: Smart hurdler; has a future on the Flat but will likely get a mark after this third start

runners.name: Humphrey
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; closely related to 1m3f Listed winner Lionel, half-brother to 1m6f Group 2 winner Gregory; dam 14.5f Group 2 winner from classy family; market highly informative on debut, particularly in relation to stablemate/previous winner Water To Wine; non-runner at Haydock on Thursday due to the ground.
runners.comment: Market highly informative on debut, particularly in relation to stablemate Water To Wine

runners.name: Sandpyper
runners.spotlight: 8,000euros yearling; 10,000gns breeze-up 2yo; twelfth foal; half-brother to four winners at up to 1m2f; dam 6f 2yo winner (including Group 2; RPR 116); already gelded; probably best watched on debut representing new training partnership.
runners.comment: Probably best watched on debut representing new training partnership

runners.name: Emerald Reine
runners.spotlight: Beat just Blue Birdie over C&D three weeks ago, having trailed in last on debut at Yarmouth; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Beat just Blue Birdie over C&D three weeks ago, having trailed in last on debut

runners.name: Flag Of Eva
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and trailed in last on debut at Nottingham 18 days ago (8.3f; 66-1); surely needs more time.
runners.comment: Slowly away and trailed in last on debut 18 days ago (8.3f; 66-1); surely needs more time

runners.name: Surrey King
runners.spotlight: Never remotely in it on debut at Newbury in October (1m) and fared no better when reappearing at Salisbury this month (1m4f); looks one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Looks one for further down the line on evidence of first two starts

runners.name: Blue Birdie
runners.spotlight: 100-1 when failing to beat a rival on debut over C&D three weeks ago; can't consider.
runners.comment: 100-1 when failing to beat a rival on debut over C&D three weeks ago; can't consider

runners.name: Noble Grace
runners.spotlight: Went close in sole bumper start at Cheltenham last April but failed to build on that in half a dozen hurdle starts since; last month's Flat debut at Wolverhampton wasn't bad (she made late headway into fourth of eight over 1m4f) but better opportunities await, probably over 1m6f+.
runners.comment: Last month's Flat debut wasn't bad but better opportunities await, probably over 1m6f+

runners.name: Ya Karim
runners.spotlight: Good third on C&D debut in September (winner subsequently won the Dante; second won his next two starts) and got the job done at Yarmouth next time (7f); fair return in quite a hot novice at that track 46 days ago, particularly as he raced freely in a first-time hood (left off here) stepping up to 1m; gelded since; has been gelded; key player on handicap debut from rail draw.
runners.comment: 2yo form reads well; return better than it looks; key player on handicap debut

runners.name: Tamashal
runners.spotlight: Raced alone to begin with but soon in trouble once the others joined on turf debut at Newbury in April; had won a Polytrack maiden (1m; readily) and a Tapeta handicap (7f) going into that so a revival back on AW is possible.
runners.comment: Blew out on turf debut latest; had won his last two on AW going into that

runners.name: Seu Jo
runners.spotlight: Struck at Chantilly in March (1m) but, along with Northern Empire and Exotic Baby, he struggled at Ascot 19 days ago when debuting in Britain for new connections (also 1m; 80-1); looks best watched.
runners.comment: Looks best watched having struggled on British debut 19 days ago

runners.name: Watcha Snoop
runners.spotlight: Drawn widest at Chester last time and was returning from 200 days off there the time before, but few positives can be gleaned; watching brief advised stepping up to 7f for AW debut.
runners.comment: Watching brief advised stepping up to 7f for AW debut

runners.name: Caviar Cowboy
runners.spotlight: Improved RPR each start, culminating in ready win on handicap debut at Lingfield last month (7f, AW); plenty went wrong for the other 2-1 joint-favourite, so that contest may not have taken much winning, but he should still be competitive off 7lb higher here.
runners.comment: Should be competitive in follow-up bid for AW win, though main rival had torrid time

runners.name: Lord Britain
runners.spotlight: Has been competitive or more when away from Group company and a strongly run race back at 7f should suit; others still appeal more from a handicapping perspective.
runners.comment: Others appeal more from a handicapping perspective

runners.name: On The Inlet
runners.spotlight: Readily won first two handicaps on Tapeta this year (1m/7f); unable to get into the same kind of rhythm back on turf last time and he's passed over in another competitive heat.
runners.comment: Came up well short in hat-trick bid returned to turf latest; passed over

runners.name: Shahik
runners.spotlight: George Boughey/Shadwell castoff after winning third and final start at two; 66-1 both starts for new connections and ran accordingly; leap of faith required to support; cheekpieces introduced last time, now removed.
runners.comment: Leap of faith required to support on 3yo evidence for new connections

runners.name: Exotic Baby
runners.spotlight: Scored under Billy Loughnane at Southwell in September (1m) and ran well under a penalty over C&D next time; smidge disappointing in two handicap runs 132 days apart before stopping quickly on turf debut 19 days ago; much safer options elsewhere; cheekpieces introduced last time, retained.
runners.comment: Stopped quickly on turf debut 19 days ago; much safer elsewhere

runners.name: King's Trust
runners.spotlight: Beat one who is now on a mark of 95 off level weights at Southwell in October (7f); blotted his copybook by playing up before breaking out of the stalls on intended return/handicap debut in 1m contest at Ascot this month (11-2 at the time of withdrawal); cheekpieces replace blinkers; has the talent to play a major role.
runners.comment: Seemingly has his quirks but could be well treated going into handicaps
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Northern Empire
runners.spotlight: Form of Lingfield novice win in January (7f) reads well but he flopped when sent off 13-2 and 9-2 in handicap company since; perhaps 6f, then racing freely over 1m when tackling turf for the first time, can partially explain those outcomes; stall 13 might make life difficult tonight.
runners.comment: Few positives to take from two handicap runs and is drawn wide tonight

runners.name: Gorey Gold
runners.spotlight: Much the most exposed of these and arrives here off the back of a particularly poor run; cheekpieces introduced last time are now removed.
runners.comment: Much the most exposed of these and arrives here off the back of a particularly poor run

runners.name: Slight Of Foot
runners.spotlight: Four siblings have triple-figure RPRs; all runs at 7f; was a promising runner-up on his second and final start at two here in September; nothing went right on his return at Wolverhampton last month and he looks worth another chance now qualified for handicaps.
runners.comment: Bred to do better and looks worth keeping onside now handicapping

races.race_name: Unibet 3 Uniboosts A Day Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Alther Walden
runners.spotlight: Patently failed to stay 2m latest (stopped quickly and was pulled up) but had been going along nicely previously, winning Catterick novice (by 14l) and Newcastle handicap at around this trip; can get back on track with Oisin Murphy doing the steering.
runners.comment: Patently failed to stay 2m latest; can get back on track with Oisin Murphy riding

runners.name: Master Vintner
runners.spotlight: Won first two starts for this yard (1m2f/1m4f) last September; had a tough first handicap assignment and he may have found it all a bit much; sent off favourite for return at Newmarket 12 days ago and was bang there over 2f out before weakening (finished behind Ernst Blofeld); dropped 3lb and reunited with Rossa Ryan; looks set to leave that run behind.
runners.comment: Won first 2 starts for the yard in novices; remains with potential and could be the answer
runners.owner: R A Pegum And Paul Anthony Henry

runners.name: Time Loop
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 5yo who again showed improved form when tackling a (hot) handicap for the first time at Newcastle in January; new trip of 1m4f raises a question but she's a danger to all if seeing it out; holds Irish Group 3 entry over this trip next month.
runners.comment: New trip of 1m4f raises a question but she's a danger to all if seeing it out

runners.name: Boatswain
runners.spotlight: Ready winner on debut last year (1m2f, Polytrack) and ran to an RPR in the 90s each start after; returned from nearly eight months off with lacklustre effort at Ascot and is suddenly best watched; cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: Suddenly best watched following lacklustre effort on reappearance
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Bulletin
runners.spotlight: Yet to finish out of the frame and his last win was achieved here; stall 14 might not be terminal over this trip but the handicapper may now have his measure.
runners.comment: High marks for consistency but the handicapper may now have his measure

runners.name: Steel Tiger
runners.spotlight: Goes well fresh (twice recorded a personal best when back from a break) and will appreciate this return to an artificial surface; fitting of a tongue-tie for this return could be a sign of intent; of interest.
runners.comment: Goes well fresh, prefers AW to turf and the fitting of a tongue-tie is interesting

runners.name: Son Of Man
runners.spotlight: High marks for consistency of late and belatedly doubled his tally at Lingfield last month (1m4f, AW); better form when going down by a nose under same conditions next time and it wasn't much of a drop off when third at Windsor last week; hard to rule out.
runners.comment: Belatedly doubled his tally then ran two good races in defeat

runners.name: Fox Avatar
runners.spotlight: Well placed by connections to win seven, eight and six-runner races (1m2f/1m4f); new mark demands more but he could find the requisite improvement now he's turned five.
runners.comment: New mark demands more but he could find the requisite improvement now he's turned five

runners.name: Ernst Blofeld
runners.spotlight: Novice win came here; signed off for the Gosdens with two poor runs but his reappearance run at Newmarket 12 days ago, following a gelding operation, was promising and strongly suggested that the new trip of 1m4f is what he wants; Billy Loughnane, who is 1-2 for the yard, takes over.
runners.comment: Has obvious potential to build on pleasing stable debut kept to 1m4f

runners.name: Kirchner
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m in France; picked up for 72,000euros by these shrewd connections and wasn't disgraced when seventh of 14 on first start for them and for nearly a year at Ascot recently (1m4f; 50-1); cheekpieces retained; should step forward but has plenty of ground to make up on the second Bulletin.
runners.comment: Should win races for his shrewd connections but might need another run or two

runners.name: Miss Tonnerre
runners.spotlight: 0-7 since winning debut but has been set some stiff tasks and there was enough promise in her last-time-out fourth (1m3f, here) to suggest she could soon double her tally; hood retained; market informative on return.
runners.comment: More competitive when dropped in grade last time; market informative on return

runners.name: Gentleman Joe
runners.spotlight: Course winner for Joseph Tuite in 2021 and scored three times (one hurdles) for Henry De Bromhead after; can't support on what's been seen, under either code, for current connections.
runners.comment: Can't support on what's been seen, under either code, for current connections

runners.name: Aqwaam
runners.spotlight: Four wins (three on AW at 1m6f and 2m) and was last seen finishing third in the 2024 Cesarewitch; has gone well over this shorter trip but can only be considered if market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Monster layoff since managing third in 2024 Cesarewitch; market to guide

runners.name: Max Mayhem
runners.spotlight: Three times down the field in Bahrain for this yard and can't support with confidence, for all moving back up in trip should help.
runners.comment: Three times down the field in Bahrain for this yard; moving back up in trip should help

races.race_name: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Senor Cortez
runners.spotlight: Scored twice (1m6f/2m) for Darryll Holland last August and made an encouraging start for his new yard when third on Lingfield AW in March; however, tailed off back on turf (which should have been okay) one month ago.
runners.comment: Lingfield AW 3rd in March; tailed off back on turf (should have been okay) one month ago

runners.name: Selenic
runners.spotlight: Won a novice here and her one handicap win was on Newcastle AW; moved up from 1m4f to 2m for warm race at Lingfield (AW) eight weeks ago and, although below best, did not appear beaten by the trip as she plugged on into fourth of 14 with first-time cheekpieces; may have more to offer as a stayer.
runners.comment: 4th in warm race at Lingfield (AW) when upped to 2m; may have more to offer as a stayer
runners.owner: Sir F Brooke & Sir R Pilkington

runners.name: Gooloogong
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in May 2025 and 2026, latterly in clearcut style (from Now The Eagle) when 6-5 favourite on return from hurdling; back up 5lb now; third in this race last year and looks set for another prominent finish.
runners.comment: C&D winner in May 2025 and 2026, latterly in clearcut style when 6-5 favourite

runners.name: High Point
runners.spotlight: Achieved little twice over hurdles before his third of five from the front over C&D two months ago when last seen; that was comfortably his best show on AW but he needs to find extra.
runners.comment: Latest start was comfortably his best show on AW but he needs to find extra

runners.name: Now The Eagle
runners.spotlight: Won in April and creditable second early this month, both over C&D under Jack Dace; Gooloogong had his measure in no uncertain style in the latter race, however, and this ex-French 4yo needs to improve on those recent runs.
runners.comment: 1st and 2nd over C&D recently; Gooloogong had his measure in no uncertain style last time

runners.name: Robusto
runners.spotlight: Often makes the running but came from towards rear under Harry Vigors to win a six-runner race at Wolverhampton (1m6f) on latest outing; that broke a long losing run but he's usually competitive and has won over C&D; player.
runners.comment: Latest start broke a long losing run but he's usually competitive and has won over C&D

runners.name: Yaa Min
runners.spotlight: Two wins from five starts since joining Hayley Burton, with both the wins over 1m4f on Lingfield Polytrack (dictated the pace on penultimate start); back to turf for a lesser show on Saturday; while not dismissed due to this new trip, it is a sizeable step in the dark.
runners.comment: 2-5 for yard; while not dismissed due to this new trip, it is a sizeable step in the dark

runners.name: Moon Chime
runners.spotlight: Bumper and pretty useful hurdle winner, including at Haydock in the prestigious 2m Swinton 18 days ago when tongue tied for the first time; Flat career of just four races, latest when fourth of eight from the back when joint-favourite on Flat handicap debut at Southwell (1m6f, AW) in April; has more to offer in this sphere and is one to note.
runners.comment: Won the prestigious Swinton over hurdles 18 days ago when tongue tied for the first time
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

runners.name: Filibustering
runners.spotlight: Tongue-tie went on for six-runner handicaps on his last two hurdle races (2m1f/2m) and he won them both; his last Flat race was in 2024 for Tim Easterby and that was a pretty solid campaign; acts on Tapeta, unraced on Polytrack; needs to be at his best on return to Flat but no shock if he's a major player.
runners.comment: Won his last two hurdle races; no shock if he's a major player on return to Flat

runners.name: Premiere Ligne
runners.spotlight: Finally off the mark in Britain in May 2024 and runner-up on next four starts, including when coming from well back over 2m4f at Glorious Goodwood; however, he flopped when just 9-1 for the Cesarewitch (soft) that October and not seen since; that was his only run in Britain on softer than good; well handicapped were he to return at his best on this AW debut after 588 days off; market may guide.
runners.comment: Well handicapped were he to return at his best on this AW debut after 588 days off

runners.name: Baltic
runners.spotlight: Won his first five handicaps (including on Tapeta, unproven on Polytrack) but the last of those was in July 2024; generally disappointing since and, after 307 days off, this is a new trip on first run since changing hands and leaving Harry Charlton.
runners.comment: 307 days off; new trip on first run since changing hands and leaving Harry Charlton

runners.name: Flying Ace
runners.spotlight: Third in an Irish point; beaten 20l in a bumper and even further on hurdle debut at Downpatrick, though did run well for a long way latterly; every chance in this weak a race.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long way on hurdle debut at Downpatrick; probably the one to beat

runners.name: Military Cross
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden for the Gosdens but suffered a few near misses at up to 1m6f; made a quiet stable debut on the Flat (tongue tied after wind surgery) but that was in a strong handicap at York; could make a hurdler.
runners.comment: Sufficient ability and stamina on the Flat to believe he could make a hurdler

runners.name: Etretat
runners.spotlight: Left Charlie Johnston for 1,000gns; 150-1 on hurdle debut at Perth and performed accordingly.
runners.comment: 150-1 on hurdle debut at Perth and performed accordingly

runners.name: Masekela
runners.spotlight: Low-grade Flat performer who joined this yard for £5,000; 25-1 and tailed off in a good-ground maiden at Hexham.
runners.comment: Low-grade Flat performer; 25-1 and tailed off in a good-ground maiden at Hexham

runners.name: Colinski
runners.spotlight: Won a Class 6 handicap on the Flat last year over 1m3f (on good ground); ran better on his hurdle debut at Hexham (good to soft) than his margin of defeat may suggest; this surface will suit him better.
runners.comment: Modest Flat winner; ran better on hurdle debut (good/soft) than margin of defeat suggests

runners.name: Big Gypsy King
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in both runs over hurdles and no worthwhile Flat form in the meantime.
runners.comment: Tailed off in both runs over hurdles and no worthwhile Flat form in the meantime

runners.name: Ribble River
runners.spotlight: No great shakes on the Flat for Kevin Ryan but did win three times at about 1m4f; however, it's been a poor start to his hurdling career.
runners.comment: Three wins on the Flat but it's been a poor start to his hurdling career
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Mrs C Clark Miss A Lee

races.race_name: In Memory Of Jimmy Latham Selling Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Alvesta
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden and no recent evidence to suggest she's about to put that right.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden and no recent evidence to suggest she's about to put that right

runners.name: Mojo Ego
runners.spotlight: His last two runs have been disappointing but he could bounce back around here where he was twice runner-up over C&D last summer.
runners.comment: Could bounce back around here where he was twice runner-up over C&D last summer

runners.name: Book Of Secrets
runners.spotlight: Only 7l away when fourth at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) in March and remains on a good mark; without a win since 2023 but has possbilities; Leah Noreci is his regular partner.
runners.comment: Only 7l away when fourth at Newcastle (2m1f) in March and remains on a good mark

runners.name: Restandbethankful
runners.spotlight: Mixes chasing with hurdling and he's stuck on a losing run; last three efforts thoroughly underwhelming.
runners.comment: Stuck on a losing run and last three efforts thoroughly underwhelming

runners.name: Mr Globalist
runners.spotlight: Yet to win in 37 runs Flat and jumps; beaten a long way in a non-handicap seller last time.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden and beaten a long way in a non-handicap seller last time

runners.name: Frank Stamper
runners.spotlight: Relegated into a seller and he has form claims, having finished runner-up on two occasions this year; however, he did run desperately only ten days ago.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up this year but he did run desperately only ten days ago

runners.name: Character Testing
runners.spotlight: Flat winner who is 0-15 over hurdles, though does have placed form off this mark; it wasn't too bad a run 19 days ago at Market Rasen.
runners.comment: Flat winner who is 0-15 over hurdles, though does have placed form off this mark

runners.name: Hashtag Queen
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced but looks limited and she's 13lb out of the weights on handicap debut; tries cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Lightly raced but looks limited and she's 13lb out of the weights on handicap debut

runners.name: Zamond
runners.spotlight: Absent since December but that was a good run at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) and he has competitive form here on good ground; one to consider.
runners.comment: Absent since December but that was a good run at Newcastle; has form here

runners.name: Justlikefire
runners.spotlight: Second to progressive rival at Uttoxeter (2m, soft) in November and running well before penultimate-flight fall next time; below that level when a remote second at Hexham but he's better than that and now goes in a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: In pretty good form until his remote second at Hexham; now goes in a tongue-tie

runners.name: Mr Rumbalicious
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in 2023; currently out form and he's 5lb out of the weights; another change of headgear.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2023; currently out form and he's 5lb out of the weights

runners.name: Silent Revolution
runners.spotlight: His good days are long gone and he has been regressing for a while; made no impact on stable debut.
runners.comment: His good days are long gone and he has been regressing for a while

runners.name: Zhang Fei
runners.spotlight: Has struggled over hurdles since rejoining this stable but his penultimate Flat run was encouraging and this is his first time in a seller.
runners.comment: His penultimate Flat run was encouraging and this is his first time in a seller

runners.name: Dickens
runners.spotlight: In a consistent run of form when last seen in November, mostly hooded but also in just a tongue-tie last time; this mark is very manageable but he does lack a recent run.
runners.comment: This mark is very manageable but he does lack a recent run

runners.name: Sea The Clouds
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of form around here over hurdles and fences, and very nearly won this race 12 months ago off 1lb higher (under Charlotte Jones); ran surprisingly well when 125-1 for a Class 2 at Haydock last time; in the mix.
runners.comment: Nearly won this last year and ran well in a competitive Class 2 last time

runners.name: Got Grey
runners.spotlight: Capable hurdler/chaser for Dan Skelton but signed off for that yard on a downer and was last of the three to finish in a chase here on Monday; unraced over this far.
runners.comment: Ex Skelton horse who was last of the three to finish over fences here on Monday

runners.name: Rauzan
runners.spotlight: A tongue-tie is added after his Kelso disappointment and his two previous runs in 2m5f/2m3f handicaps certainly bring him into the equation; unraced here.
runners.comment: Below par last time but previous efforts firmly bring him into the equation

runners.name: Lowlands
runners.spotlight: Consistent mare who made all in a 2m4f maiden at Perth (good) and therefore brings momentum into her handicap return; this mark could be okay.
runners.comment: Made all in a Perth maiden so brings momentum into her handicap return

runners.name: Rioja Alta
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 2lb lower last summer (soft) and some respectable runs in the meantime; the latest at Perth wasn't one of them but he's hard to rule out.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 2lb lower last summer (soft) and some respectable runs in the meantime

runners.name: Beny Nahar Road
runners.spotlight: Better chaser than hurdler but handicapped accordingly and has placed form in this discipline; however, he has been off since falling over fences in September.
runners.comment: Capable 7yo but he has been off since falling over fences in September

runners.name: Swingingonthesteel
runners.spotlight: The winner of three bumpers, the first in points; beaten at odds-on in his first maiden hurdle and has shown nothing since, so goes handicapping with much to prove.
runners.comment: Won bumpers but disappointing so far over hurdles; makes handicap debut here

runners.name: Musique De Fee
runners.spotlight: Second here on Saturday and third on Monday in first-time blinkers, though she was some 22l off the winner; opposable should she run yet again.
runners.comment: Second here on Saturday and third on Monday in first-time blinkers; beaten 22l latterly

runners.name: Bittalemon
runners.spotlight: The winner of two 2m1f novice events here (good to soft/good) last summer; however, she dropped out very tamely in a 3m1f handicap here on Saturday.
runners.comment: Dual course winner but dropped out very tamely in a 3m1f handicap here on Saturday

runners.name: Hillview
runners.spotlight: Had struggled after returning from a long absence but last time was a bit better and Sean Bowen now takes over; could be interesting, for all that he's not shone around here in the past.
runners.comment: Had struggled after returning from a long absence but last time was a bit better

races.race_name: Join Rebecca Menzies Racing Club Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Dusky Days
runners.spotlight: After a break he's been tailed off behind Wee Alki and here on Monday faded into sixth over 2m6f; others preferred.
runners.comment: After a break he's been tailed off behind Wee Alki and here on Monday faded into sixth

runners.name: Secret Secret
runners.spotlight: Three course wins over 2m6f; below par in two races before finishing fifth behind Evenwood Sonofagun here on Monday; has to step up.
runners.comment: Goes well here but was only fifth behind Evenwood Sonofagun here on Monday

runners.name: Wee Alki
runners.spotlight: Doubled his hurdle tally over nearly 3m at Hexham this month and that was on the back of good runs in defeat; raised 6lb but has placed form under the returning Rebekah Duffy who claims 7lb.
runners.comment: Thriving 7yo who looks sure to put up another good account of himself

runners.name: Evenwood Sonofagun
runners.spotlight: Won the 2m6f handicap on this card 12 months ago and returned to form with a 2l success over that same trip on Monday (following a wind operation); did that comfortably and must have every chance under a penalty.
runners.comment: Comfortable winner here on Monday and must have every chance with a penalty
runners.owner: Mrs D Chapman & Mrs E Quinlan

runners.name: Jack Sprat
runners.spotlight: Capable on his day for Jamie Snowden, winning three times; didn't sign off for that yard in bad form and he joined this one for £12,000; could go well.
runners.comment: Capable on his day for Jamie Snowden, winning three times; recruited for £12,000

runners.name: Ebselysees
runners.spotlight: Ex-Noel Williams mare; since a quiet yard debut she's won 3m/3m2f handicaps at Perth (good/good to soft), the latest by a comfortable 8l; raised another 7lb but something has evidently clicked.
runners.comment: Something has clicked after back-to-back wins at Perth; feared after a 7lb rise

runners.name: I Am Spider Man
runners.spotlight: Nearly two years since he won a race but arrives here after solid seconds in handicaps at about 3m2f; surprising to see the hood missing but he's still much respected.
runners.comment: Nearly two years since he won a race but arrives here after solid seconds

runners.name: Defying Gravity
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo; in this new headgear combination when a late mistake (and some wandering) cost him a 3m chase at Perth and again he showed some quirks when ducking right and unseating at the first fence at Kelso on Sunday; capable of having a say on this return to hurdling but comes with an element of risk.
runners.comment: Near miss over fences two runs back; unseated early on Sunday; has his quirks

runners.name: Poetry Man
runners.spotlight: After three successive pull-ups over fences he got back on track when close up in a 2m6f handicap hurdle here on Monday when in a first-time visor; if turned out again he'd command a level of respect.
runners.comment: Third here on Monday and if turned out again he'd command a level of respect

runners.name: Kingofthegame
runners.spotlight: A fading third over fences here on Saturday (2m5f, good) when all headgear was left off; back hurdling off 1lb higher (out of the weights) and stamina could be stretched over this far.
runners.comment: Third over fences on Saturday (2m5f); his stamina could be stretched over this far

runners.name: Gustavo Goodway
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m6f handicap hurdle when trained in Ireland but was pulled up in three successive handicaps last year for Joanne Foster; opposable, despite since winning two small-field points since.
runners.comment: Recently won a couple of small-field points but he's opposable back under rules

races.race_name: Birchall Trust Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hostile Hotelier
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner who is 0-6 over fences (despite a lower mark) and was beaten a good way at Hexham (3m) on his return to this discipline after a break; the other two appeal more.
runners.comment: Four-time hurdle winner who is 0-6 over fences despite a lower mark

runners.name: Off The Jury
runners.spotlight: Had three wind operations, the latest prior to him chasing home the favourite in a maiden hurdle at Perth a month ago (2m4f); he nearly won last year's EBF Final at Sandown when with Alan King, so has some class about him; won his only point and should do well over fences.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler who was second in a maiden last time; should make a chaser

runners.name: Caughtinyourtrance
runners.spotlight: Both wins over hurdles were here; runner-up in two of his four chase starts, the latest here on Saturday when no match for the winner over 3m1f; fine over this far and has clear claims.
runners.comment: Dual winner here over hurdles and ran second over fences here on Saturday

runners.name: Ghasham
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdle winner who left Dan Skelton for 4,400gns; finished a clear second at Hexham (1m7f, good to soft; effective on good) on stable/chase debut and the revitalised winner has struck again since; gets in here off the same mark.
runners.comment: Second on stable\chase debut and the winner has gone in again since; same mark

runners.name: Zumbi
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won for ages but nearly put that right when going down by a neck here on Saturday (2m5f, good); Leah Noreci rode then so he's without the 10lb claim this time; still respected with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Hasn't won for ages but nearly put that right when going down by a neck here on Saturday

runners.name: Clean Getaway
runners.spotlight: Usually saves his best for Sedgefield, where he won over fences in April (subsequent fourth was over hurdles); two visits here has produced a second place; no surprise to see him run well.
runners.comment: Usually saves his best for Sedgefield but two visits here has produced a second place

runners.name: Beat The Edge
runners.spotlight: Extremely hard to win with and modest runs outweigh the good ones these days.
runners.comment: Extremely hard to win with and modest runs outweigh the good ones these days

runners.name: Cuzco Du Mathan
runners.spotlight: Not in flying form earlier this year but all three career wins have been at Cartmel, the last two over fences; the concern is the conditions as he does enjoy significant cut underfoot.
runners.comment: All three career wins have been here, though on soft and heavy ground

runners.name: Starman
runners.spotlight: Close second over fences at Catterick in January (1m7f, good) but mainly poor efforts since, the latest in a selling hurdle.
runners.comment: Close second over fences at Catterick in January but mainly poor efforts since

runners.name: Prospect House
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on his only previous run here and returns on the back of a third chase win, gained at Catterick in December (1m7f, good to soft; fine on good); if fit enough then he should be competitive off a fair mark.
runners.comment: Successful when last seen in December; if fit enough he should be on the scene
runners.owner: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing

runners.name: Bingo Babe
runners.spotlight: 0-21 with his best efforts coming over 5f at Sha Tin; 5lb out of the weights and hard to fancy from his wide draw.
runners.comment: 0-21 with his best efforts coming over 5f at Sha Tin; hard to fancy from his wide draw
runners.owner: Charmaine Wong Kam Mei & Chan Fu Ho

runners.name: Exceed The Wish
runners.spotlight: 0-17 and stepping up in trip hasn't made a different lately, but he is 10lb lower than when narrowly beaten in a Class 4 over C&D in October; Zac Purton rides him for the first time; respected.
runners.comment: 0-17, but now 10lb lower than when narrowly beaten in a Class 4 over C&D in October

runners.name: Winning Diamond
runners.spotlight: 0-11 and has been running over further so far this year; not sure the return to this trip is a positive.
runners.comment: 0-11; has been running over further this year; not sure the return to 6f is a positive

runners.name: Basic Instinct
runners.spotlight: 1-4 in New Zealand but 0-15 in Hong Kong; only beaten around 3l into sixth at Sha Tin (6f) last time and would have gone closer with a clearer run (third has won since); has the plum draw and interesting if building on that.
runners.comment: Only beaten around 3l at Sha Tin last time and would have gone closer with a clearer run
runners.owner: 22/23 Me Tsui Yu Sak Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Gimme Five
runners.spotlight: 1-27 with the win coming on dirt; placed four times over C&D, but recent efforts not encouraging.
runners.comment: 1-27; placed four times over C&D, but recent efforts not encouraging

runners.name: Von Baer
runners.spotlight: 2-8 in Britain, but 0-20 in Hong Kong; drop to this level and a change of stable has made little difference and he has a high draw to boot.
runners.comment: 0-20 in Hong Kong; drop to this level and a change of stable has made little difference

runners.name: Sportic Warrior
runners.spotlight: No closer than ninth in 13 starts over a variety of trips; makes little appeal from 6lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: No closer than ninth in 13 starts over a variety of trips; makes little appeal
runners.owner: Edmund Wong Yan Ming, Michael Lee To Ming Et Al

runners.name: Noble Deluxe
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come over 6f on dirt at Sha Tin this year, but he didn't run very well on that surface three weeks ago and a record of 0-18 on turf makes him hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Both wins have come on dirt at Sha Tin; record of 0-18 on turf makes him hard to recommend

runners.name: Flying Christie
runners.spotlight: No closer than tenth in seven starts in a multitude of headgear, but the drop in class and a good draw are small reasons for optimism.
runners.comment: No show in seven starts but a drop in class and a good draw are small reasons for optimism

runners.name: Solar River
runners.spotlight: 0-12 and made little impact on his reappearance five weeks ago, but he drops into a Class 5 for the first time and could be given a chance on his two placed efforts over C&D around this time last year.
runners.comment: 0-12, but down in class and ran well a couple of times over C&D last year

runners.name: Brave Win
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in all nine starts and not the best of draws, though he could be more competitive now dropped into a Class 5.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in all nine starts, though could be more competitive dropped to Class 5
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Matthew Lam Kin Hong

runners.name: Happy Action
runners.spotlight: 0-12 and best efforts so far have come on dirt at Sha Tin; opposable from the outside stall.
runners.comment: 0-12 and best efforts so far have come on dirt; opposable from the outside stall

runners.name: Thousand Cups
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 0-12, but placed a couple of times over C&D including when beaten half a length last month; drawn wide when not so good last time, but would be considered if getting a run and better berthed.
runners.comment: 0-12, but placed a couple of times over C&D including when beaten half a length last month
runners.owner: Tsang Kam Chuen, Simon Siu Chak Yu Et Al

runners.name: Lucky Generations
runners.spotlight: Reserve; few marks for consistency, but he has won twice over C&D since December so would be worth a place on the shortlist if getting a run.
runners.comment: Has won twice over C&D since December so would be worth a place on the shortlist

runners.name: Mega Force
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in all 18 starts in Hong Kong including three over C&D; best watched.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in all 18 starts in Hong Kong including three over C&D; best watched

runners.name: Perfect Pairing
runners.spotlight: 1-32 locally, but the win did come over C&D while he has finished runner-up three times from seven other starts over the trip; could go well.
runners.comment: 1-32 locally, but the win did come over C&D; could go well
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Jerry Chow Shiu Ming

runners.name: Mr Aladdin
runners.spotlight: Has run some good races around here including over this trip, but now 0-35 and opposable from 4lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: 0-35 and opposable from 4lb out of the weights

runners.name: Golden Fairy
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has winning form over C&D and is 4lb lower than when last successful; will need to improve on recent efforts, though.
runners.comment: C&D winner and 4lb lower than when last successful; needs to improve on recent efforts
runners.owner: Rex Wong Siu Han, Ivan Wong Siu Kei & Ann Wong Pui Gee

runners.name: Chateau Le Peche
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 1-27; 7lb lower than for his sole win at Sha Tin (1m2f) just over a year ago, but his recent form has been regressive and he will need more if getting a run.
runners.comment: 1-27; recent form has been regressive and he will need more if getting a run

runners.name: Phantom Cyclone
runners.spotlight: 0-23 in Hong Kong and his best efforts have come on dirt; didn't run very well in one previous go over this far and others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: 0-23 in Hong Kong; didn't run very well in one previous go over this far

runners.name: Rosewood Fleetfoot
runners.spotlight: 0-14, but was only beaten a length at Sha Tin (1m1f) ten days ago; races off the same mark, but he has finished out of the frame in all three starts over C&D.
runners.comment: 0-14; close second at Sha Tin last time, but out of the frame in all three starts over C&D
runners.owner: Lam Ka Lai & Yeung Wing Shut

runners.name: Smart Beauty
runners.spotlight: All five wins have come at Sha Tin (1m/1m1f); unplaced in all nine starts since his latest success and the longer trip poses another question.

runners.name: Embrace Aberdeen
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo who has collected two 6f Class 4 wins at Sha Tin this season; ended up poorly positioned on outside of the field when only sixth over C&D last month and may still have more to offer.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 3yo with 2-6 strike-rate; may still have more to offer; shortlisted

runners.name: Robot Lucky Star
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won at Sha Tin in October and was second over C&D twice in November; returned from break with underwhelming run last month but will be dangerous from stall 1 if back on song.
runners.comment: Made low-key reappearance last month but will be dangerous from stall 1 if back on song

runners.name: Flying Hunter
runners.spotlight: Towards rear throughout when big-priced outsider for first two Hong Kong starts (both 6f); cannot be recommended from stall 12.
runners.comment: Towards rear throughout when big-priced outsider for first two Hong Kong starts

runners.name: Sky Cap
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 4yo who won consecutive C&D starts in March/April and was placed over 5f when back in this higher grade last month; not discredited when fifth from double-digit draw over C&D a week ago and remains of interest.
runners.comment: Resurgent 4yo; won twice over C&D in March/April and ran well from tough draw last week

runners.name: Son Pak Fu
runners.spotlight: Seven-time C&D winner; no longer the force of old but appreciated ease in grade when second over C&D a fortnight ago and gets another chance off the same mark today.
runners.comment: Appreciated ease in grade when second over C&D two weeks ago; same mark today

runners.name: Matters Most
runners.spotlight: Ran well from the front when second over C&D two starts ago, but had the advantageous inside draw that day and this year's record is very mixed; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Had inside draw when second over C&D two runs ago; this year's record is very mixed

runners.name: Sports Legend
runners.spotlight: Never really looked dangerous over C&D last month and remains 7lb higher than when winning here in December; opposable from stall 11.
runners.comment: Never really looked dangerous here last time; opposable from stall 11
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Boniface Ho Ka Kui

runners.name: Lucky Planet
runners.spotlight: Reserve; just held on to make all over C&D in February, his second win this season, but recent efforts suggest he's too high in the weights now.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season but seemingly too high in the weights now
runners.owner: Leung Kai Fai & Cheung Sui Ling
